rows.imp_rat: Components inaccessible for examination at delivery is a receiving-inspection limitation, marginal to operation with minor consequences absorbed at intake.
rows.sat_rat: A meaningful fraction of components stays inside sealed crates or factory-installed guards, so some remain unexamined at delivery. Scheduled delivery inspections give reasonable time to walk the equipment, though packed sub-assemblies slow full access. Trained receiving inspectors and vendor reps can access most components, but tightly crated internals need supplier assistance. Uncrating and re-crating for full inspection adds handling cost that is often skipped to save money.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of anchor holes drilled outside the required mounting locations
rows.imp_rat: Anchor holes drilled outside required mounting locations is an installation rework error, secondary to the machine's cartoning function (very low relevance) with correctable but costly rework (low-moderate severity).
rows.sat_rat: Drilling templates and marked layouts make correct hole placement fairly consistent, with occasional misplacements. Using templates or drill jigs adds minor setup time but speeds accurate hole placement. Accurate anchor drilling relies on installer care and basic layout skill that competent crews possess. Misdrilled holes waste effort and require patching, a modest recurring cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of initial meter readings recorded incorrectly
rows.imp_rat: Incorrectly recorded initial meter readings is a data-entry defect at setup with little bearing on cartoning operation (very low relevance) and minor downstream tracking impact (low severity). Within 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: Manual meter recording is error-prone, though the low complexity keeps most readings acceptably accurate. Recording initial meter readings is a quick task at setup. Reading and logging meters needs minimal skill, so errors are transcription slips. Incorrect baseline readings are cheap to make but costly to trace later.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time between completing a maintenance event and recording it in the machine history
rows.imp_rat: Delay between completing maintenance and recording it in history is a bookkeeping timeliness issue peripheral to the core job (low relevance) with trivial, easily absorbed consequences (very low severity). Capped at 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: Immediate electronic logging keeps most entries timely, but deferred end-of-shift recording still introduces memory-based inaccuracies. With mobile CMMS entry the lag between finishing work and recording it is short, though paper backlogs still delay some sites. Prompt recording is a discipline matter, not a skill barrier. Delayed recording carries little direct cost beyond occasional data-integrity questions.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of cartoning machine unavailability caused by daily cleaning
rows.imp_rat: Downtime from daily cleaning is expected routine unavailability, only loosely central and with trivial per-instance consequences, held to the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Established cleaning SOPs and tool-less quick-clean designs make daily cleaning a consistent, predictable activity. Daily cleaning stoppages are routine and their duration is well understood, though changeover and cleaning still consume real uptime. Cleaning is largely operator-level work with clear SOPs, requiring little specialist skill. The recurring daily uptime loss is a known, budgeted cost of operation.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of stocked spare parts rendered unusable by storage damage or contamination
rows.imp_rat: Spare parts ruined by storage damage is a maintenance-logistics error only tangential to the machine's core cartoning function (very low relevance); consequences are absorbable replacement costs (low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Controlled stores with shelving, climate control and FIFO rotation are common practice, though contamination and shelf-life lapses still occur in less disciplined stores. Discovering a damaged spare at point of use forces a scramble that extends downtime. Proper storage of seals, elastomers and electronics needs some know-how but is largely procedural. Damaged or expired parts are direct scrap losses plus the cost of re-procurement.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the transferred machine title remains subject to an undisclosed lien or security interest
rows.imp_rat: An undisclosed lien on transferred title is a legal-ownership defect remote from cartoning operation; while it can complicate ownership, it is peripheral to the CFJ. Capped by the batch ranking.
rows.sat_rat: UCC/lien search practice is mature, so undisclosed encumbrances rarely survive a proper transfer. Lien searches add days but are routine within asset-transfer workflows. Clearing title requires legal/finance expertise that established transactions engage. Lien searches and releases carry modest transactional cost.

rows.source_job: Isolate energy sources on the machine
rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to resolve inaccessible or defective isolation points before removal
rows.imp_rat: Time to resolve defective isolation points before removal is a decommissioning-logistics delay barely related to the cartoning CFJ (very low relevance) with modest schedule cost (low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Ad hoc repair approaches vary widely, so the time to resolve is inconsistent and hard to predict. Resolving seized or defective isolation points is a common, unpredictable delay with few fast remedies. Freeing or bypassing a defective isolation point safely needs experienced maintenance judgement. The labor and hold time to fix bad isolation points add cost but are usually contained.

rows.source_job: Erase proprietary data from machine controller
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that records subject to retention obligations are erased before preservation
rows.imp_rat: Erasing records subject to retention before preservation is a records-governance error essentially unrelated to the cartoning function, though it carries some compliance risk, still capped by ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Retention holds and archiving policies are established, but ad hoc deletion during equipment retirement still happens. Preserving retention-obligated records is quick when procedures are followed but flagging can lag decommissioning. Knowing which records carry retention obligations requires records-management judgement not always present on the floor. Premature erasure risks regulatory penalties that dwarf storage costs.

rows.source_job: Erase proprietary data from machine controller
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the machine is transferred or scrapped before controller data erasure is completed
rows.imp_rat: Failure to erase controller data before transfer or scrap is an end-of-life data-security concern unrelated to the cartoning job itself (very low relevance), though a data leak could carry some consequence (low severity). Held at or below 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: Few facilities have a gating control tying disposal release to completed erasure, so the sequencing failure is common. Erasure is often the last thing considered and machines are frequently moved out before it happens. Coordinating erasure before transfer needs someone owning OT-security accountability, a role often unassigned at disposal. Premature transfer with intact data creates outsized IP and compliance cost against negligible avoidance cost.

rows.source_job: Erase proprietary data from machine controller
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that completion of controller data erasure cannot be demonstrated to security, quality, and regulatory approvers
rows.imp_rat: Inability to demonstrate controller data erasure to approvers is a decommissioning compliance-evidence gap disconnected from the operating carton job (very low relevance), with modest audit consequences (low severity). Capped at 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: Standardized, demonstrable erasure certificates for OT devices barely exist today, so proving completion to approvers is chronically unreliable. Producing erasure evidence is an afterthought that delays sign-off when demanded at all. Generating audit-grade proof of controller erasure requires specialized tooling and expertise that is largely absent. Inability to demonstrate erasure risks failed audits and rework, though evidence capture is inexpensive if planned.

rows.stk: Sustainability Officer / Environmental Health and Safety Specialist
rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of cartoning machine components assigned to an incompatible recovery stream
rows.imp_rat: Assigning components to an incompatible recovery stream is an end-of-life recycling defect unrelated to producing cartons (very low relevance), carrying modest environmental/compliance impact (low severity). Kept at or below 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: General recycling practice exists, but complex composite machine components are frequently misassigned to incompatible streams. Sorting components into recovery streams adds handling time that scrap processors partly absorb. Assigning mixed-material machine parts to the right recovery stream needs materials knowledge often lacking at disposal. Wrong-stream assignment loses recovery value and can incur reprocessing charges.

rows.stk: Sustainability Officer / Environmental Health and Safety Specialist
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of routing recyclable cartoning machine components to facilities not authorized to accept them
rows.imp_rat: Routing recyclables to unauthorized facilities is an end-of-life disposal defect disconnected from cartoning (very low relevance) with limited compliance exposure (low severity). Bounded to 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: Vetted recycler networks and downstream audits reduce misrouting, though verification gaps persist for lower-value scrap. Verifying a recycler's authorization adds procurement time that is usually manageable. Confirming facility authorization requires waste-management diligence that established brokers handle. Routing to unauthorized facilities risks liability far above the vetting cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required maintenance, calibration, compliance, and operational records missing from the retired cartoning machine archive
rows.imp_rat: Missing records from a retired-machine archive is an end-of-life documentation error with minimal impact on active cartoning and contained consequences. Capped by batch position.
rows.sat_rat: Full maintenance/calibration/compliance archives are commonly incomplete for machines nearing end of life. Reconstructing a complete retired-machine archive is slow when records were never centralized. Completing the archive needs staff who understand which records the machine's lifecycle generated. Gaps force costly reconstruction or write-offs of the asset's documented value.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of archived machine records becoming unreadable or corrupted before the retention obligation ends
rows.imp_rat: Archived records becoming unreadable is a data-preservation reliability issue unrelated to the cartoning function, with limited compliance consequence, capped low per ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Media degradation and obsolete formats still corrupt archives despite backup practices. Format migration and integrity checks take time but the loss manifests years later. Long-term digital preservation requires archival IT skills seldom dedicated to retired machines. Migrating and maintaining readable archives adds ongoing storage and IT expense.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of archived machine records assigned an incorrect retention requirement
rows.imp_rat: Incorrect retention requirement on archived records is an administrative filing defect essentially irrelevant to cartoning function, with modest compliance consequence, held at ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Retention taxonomies exist but manual assignment produces a meaningful error rate. Assigning correct retention classes is not time-critical but errors surface much later. Correct classification demands specialized retention-schedule knowledge that is unevenly applied. Misclassification causes either costly over-retention or risky early disposal.

rows.source_job: Remove machine anchors and foundation fixtures
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging concealed floor utilities beneath the cartoning-machine foundation during anchor extraction
rows.imp_rat: Damaging concealed floor utilities during anchor extraction is a decommissioning hazard peripheral to the cartoning CFJ (very low relevance); though facility damage could be costly (moderate severity), ranking caps it at 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: With incomplete facility records the strike risk during blind anchor removal remains high and poorly mitigated today. Locating concealed utilities before anchor extraction adds delay, but the real failure is a sudden strike rather than a schedule slip. Reliable avoidance depends on scarce experienced riggers and accurate as-built drawings that are frequently missing or outdated. A struck conduit or drain line triggers expensive facility repairs and downtime far exceeding the extraction cost.

rows.stk: EHS coordinator, maintenance technician, facilities manager
rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of waste containers or shipment records missing required identification for materials removed from the cartoning machine
rows.imp_rat: Missing identification on waste containers or shipment records is a waste-disposal documentation defect unrelated to carton forming (very low relevance), risking minor regulatory tracking gaps (low severity). Within the 2.0 ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Manifest and labeling systems are mature, but incomplete or mismatched records still occur under manual data entry. Labeling containers and completing shipment records is a routine clerical step in the manifest process. Applying required identification follows checklists that need only modest training. Missing IDs cause rejected shipments and rework, a moderate cost driver.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required warehouse caser consumables are unavailable when scheduled service is due
rows.imp_rat: Unavailable consumables when scheduled service is due is a supply-logistics error tangential to the CFJ (low relevance) with minor, deferrable impact (very low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Min/max reorder points cover common consumables, but demand spikes and poor forecasting still cause occasional shortfalls at service time. Stockouts of consumables can stall a scheduled service until replenishment arrives. Managing consumable stock needs routine inventory discipline rather than specialized expertise. Consumables are low-cost, so buffer stocking is cheap and mostly practiced.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the backup is unreadable when restoration is required
rows.imp_rat: An unreadable backup at restoration time is a contingency-only failure far from routine casing (very low relevance), though painful when it hits (low severity), and is capped by the batch ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Backups are seldom test-restored, so their readability is largely unverified until the moment it is needed — a classic latent, unmitigated failure. An unreadable backup is discovered only during restoration, causing severe unplanned delay. Detecting a corrupt or incompatible backup requires controls expertise that few maintenance staff exercise routinely. Verified backup storage is inexpensive, yet the failure cost when restoration fails is very high.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the backup does not reflect the latest approved controller configuration
rows.imp_rat: A backup not reflecting the latest approved controller configuration is an IT/config-hygiene error remote from the casing job (very low relevance), with impact only surfacing on rare restores (low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Config backups are often taken ad hoc rather than tied to change control, so the stored copy frequently lags the latest approved version. Taking a fresh backup is fast; the failure is that it simply isn't done after each approved change. Backing up a controller config is a routine procedure requiring modest controls familiarity. Backups cost almost nothing, so the barrier is process discipline rather than money.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that conflicting condition limits cause inconsistent classification of warehouse caser degradation
rows.imp_rat: Conflicting condition limits causing inconsistent degradation classification is a maintenance-definition ambiguity (low relevance) with modest consequence (severity), held at 2.0 by the cap.
rows.sat_rat: Condition limits are often scattered across manuals, SOPs, and CMMS with no single reconciled source, so inconsistent degradation calls are a common, poorly-mitigated problem. Reconciling conflicting condition limits is a judgement task that rarely delays the physical work itself. Interpreting overlapping or contradictory limits demands experienced technicians who understand the equipment's real degradation behavior. Inconsistent classification wastes effort on misdirected repairs but has modest direct cost impact.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the caser remains energizable by unauthorized personnel during storage
rows.imp_rat: Unauthorized energization during storage is a peripheral concern to the casing job itself (low relevance), and though consequences could be harmful, the ceiling holds severity at 2.
rows.sat_rat: LOTO and access controls cover most storage cases, but stored idle units are sometimes left inconsistently secured or informally re-energized. Applying isolation and lockout for stored equipment is a quick one-time action. Preventing unauthorized energization uses standard lockout practice needing little special skill. Locks and tags are cheap, so cost is not a barrier to controlling storage energization.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the stored caser obstructs material routes or emergency access
rows.imp_rat: A stored caser obstructing routes or emergency access is a storage/logistics safety concern peripheral to the casing job; relevance minimal though severity nominally elevated, both capped.
rows.sat_rat: Facility layout rules and 5S practices usually keep routes clear, though space pressure occasionally leads to obstructive temporary storage. Positioning a stored caser clear of routes is a one-time placement decision with little time cost. Keeping aisles and egress clear is basic warehouse discipline, not a specialized skill. Designated storage space has an opportunity cost but no direct material expense.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the caser being damaged during removal, loading, or transportation to the recipient
rows.imp_rat: Caser damage during removal/loading/transport is an end-of-life logistics event unrelated to ongoing casing (very low relevance), though damage cost can be notable (low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Established transport practices keep damage relatively rare, though road transit shocks still occasionally harm the unit. Protective handling and load securing add predictable time to the process. Standard transport rigging skills cover protecting the caser during removal and haulage. Damage in transit devalues the asset for the recipient, adding cost, but protective packing is affordable.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of classifying spent caser lubricants, fluids, or batteries under the wrong disposal requirements
rows.imp_rat: Misclassifying spent lubricants/fluids/batteries carries compliance stakes but is a disposal-phase error far from core casing; relevance minimal, severity kept low under the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Established waste-profiling practice and SDS references make correct classification common, though unusual fluids still get misassigned. Classifying lubricants, fluids, and batteries per hazardous-waste rules adds modest time when profiles are established. Correct classification requires hazardous-waste and regulatory knowledge, typically from an EHS specialist rather than the disposal crew. Misclassification risks fines and penalties that far exceed the cost of proper profiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of regulated caser waste being transferred to a party or destination not authorized to handle it
rows.imp_rat: Transferring regulated waste to an unauthorized party has real compliance severity, but as a disposal-phase error it is peripheral to casing and ranked below the prior batch; both capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Chain-of-custody manifesting and permitted-facility verification are well-established and legally enforced, so misrouting to unauthorized parties is uncommon where the practice is followed. Manifesting and permitted-hauler scheduling add administrative time but are routine and rarely a bottleneck. Mature EPA/RCRA manifest and licensed-transporter frameworks let trained EHS staff apply established procedures without exceptional expertise. Using authorized processors costs more than general disposal but is a budgeted, predictable expense.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual material remaining in concealed or inaccessible warehouse caser areas after clearing
rows.imp_rat: Residual material left in inaccessible areas after clearing is a cleaning-thoroughness issue with limited impact on core casing throughput; relevance is minimal and severity low under the batch cap.
rows.sat_rat: Manual clearing routinely leaves residue in inaccessible cavities, so complete clearing is inconsistent. Clearing concealed pockets thoroughly is slow and often revisited when residual material is later found. Knowing where material hides inside a caser requires familiarity with its geometry that generalist crews often lack. Leftover material can require rehandling and drives contamination-related rework costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of removed lubricants and fluids assigned to incompatible waste streams
rows.imp_rat: Assigning removed fluids to incompatible waste streams is a disposal-compliance error with regulatory consequence but negligible relevance to active casing; both proxies held low.
rows.sat_rat: Labeled containers and waste manifests keep most fluids in correct streams, with occasional cross-contamination errors. Segregating fluids into compatible streams adds only minor handling time. Basic waste-stream segregation is a routine skill for licensed waste handlers. Assigning fluids to incompatible streams incurs disposal surcharges and rejected-load fees, the primary loss here.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous electrical components remaining in ordinary recyclable caser-material loads at processor handoff
rows.imp_rat: Hazardous electrical components in recyclable loads is a disposal-safety error with compliance stakes but minimal relevance to core casing; both proxies held low per ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Without a controlled disassembly checklist, hazardous electricals slip into recyclable loads with some regularity, making this a real recurring gap. Manual component-by-component inspection to pull electrical hazards adds meaningful time to teardown staging. Identifying which electrical components carry capacitors, batteries or hazardous materials requires technician judgement that is inconsistently available on disposal crews. Missed components trigger load rejection or contamination penalties, but the components themselves are low-value.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of separated caser materials rejected by approved processors because of pharmaceutical residues, lubricants, or cleaning chemicals
rows.imp_rat: Separated materials rejected by processors for residues is an end-of-life recycling issue with minor cost consequence and little bearing on the core casing function; both proxies minimal.
rows.sat_rat: Processors reject inconsistently because residue thresholds are rarely verified before handoff, so this remains a frequent, poorly-controlled failure in pharma decommissioning. Decontamination-then-verification cycles add substantial lead time before materials can be presented, and rework loops when residues are found stretch the timeline further. Reliably stripping pharmaceutical residues, lubricants and cleaning chemicals to processor-acceptable levels demands specialized decontamination know-how that most disposal crews lack. Rejected loads must be re-cleaned, re-tested or landfilled at hazardous-waste rates, so failures carry real recurring cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of facility utility lines or integration points damaged during caser removal
rows.imp_rat: Damage to facility utility lines during caser removal is a decommissioning-boundary error outside the operating CFJ (very low relevance), with contained repair consequences (low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Isolation procedures help, but poorly documented or shared connections lead to lines being nicked during removal with some regularity. Locating and safely disconnecting utility lines slows removal when documentation is incomplete. Identifying and detaching integration points requires facilities and trades knowledge of the specific hookups. Damaged utility lines force repairs and can disrupt neighboring operations at real cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of interrupted case loading during alignment correction
rows.imp_rat: Interrupted case loading during alignment correction is a short-duration throughput loss (relevance) with trivial recoverable consequences (low severity), capped at 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: These interruptions are fairly routine and brief but recur across a run. Interrupted loading during alignment correction is a direct dwell that reduces case output. Fast recovery depends on operator familiarity with clearing the loading station. The interruption costs run time more than materials.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of caser feed disruption caused by correcting item position
rows.imp_rat: Feed disruption from correcting item position is a brief throughput hiccup (relevance) with minor recoverable impact (low severity), held under the 2.0 cap.
rows.sat_rat: Correction-induced stops are somewhat unpredictable but generally short with well-maintained equipment. Feed disruptions from position correction directly stop product flow and are a recognized throughput loss. Minimizing disruption duration depends on quick operator response and setup skill to clear and restart. Downtime consumes labor and lost production capacity but not much material.

rows.source_job: Position empty cases at loading station
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of empty cases being misaligned at the caser loading station
rows.imp_rat: Misaligned empty cases at the loading station is a minor feed-quality issue (relevance) with easily corrected consequences (low severity), well within the 2.0 ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Case formers and alignment guides at the loading station are mature and keep empties squared most of the time, with occasional jams. A misaligned empty case can stall loading briefly until cleared. Case squaring is handled by mechanical formers/guides rather than skilled labor. A misaligned empty rarely wastes material unless it jams and damages product.

rows.source_job: Record cases produced per production run
rows.stmt: Reduce the number of production runs with no completed-case total recorded
rows.imp_rat: A missing completed-case total is a data-capture gap; peripheral to physical casing (low relevance) though it can complicate reconciliation, with severity capped at 2.
rows.sat_rat: Automatic counters that latch a total at run end have made fully missing totals fairly uncommon on modern casers, though manual lines still drop them. A missing total forces a time-consuming reconstruction after the run. Ensuring a total is captured depends on operator discipline at run end rather than automation on many lines. Missing records force rework of counts but no direct material loss.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of startup attempts delayed by cases being unavailable at the caser infeed
rows.imp_rat: Startup attempts delayed by unavailable cases is a minor launch-timing nuisance (relevance) with trivial consequences (low severity), capped at 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: Pre-run staging practices usually have cases ready, so delayed startups are occasional rather than routine. Startup delays from missing cases at infeed push back run start, a direct time loss. Ensuring cases are staged before startup is a routine prep task needing little skill. Delayed startups waste scheduled line time.

rows.source_job: Inspect completed cases for packing quality
rows.stmt: Reduce the rate of acceptable completed cases incorrectly classified as defective
rows.imp_rat: Misclassifying acceptable cases as defective causes only minor yield waste (very low severity) and is peripheral to completing the casing job (low relevance).
rows.sat_rat: Standard camera and sensor reject gates exist but overly conservative settings routinely bin good cases, so consistency here has real recurring gaps. Vision-based reject systems classify at line speed so time is rarely the bottleneck for this need. Setting acceptance thresholds tight enough to catch real defects without rejecting good cases still requires experienced technicians who tune systems by trial and error. False rejects of acceptable cases waste finished product and packaging material, a recurring loss operators tolerate.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of material acquisition, installation, validation, and ownership cost elements omitted from machine comparisons
rows.imp_rat: Omitting cost elements from machine comparisons is a procurement-analysis defect, peripheral to the running job with only trivial financial-decision consequences.
rows.sat_rat: Machine comparisons routinely omit cost elements inconsistently across bidders, so the comparison itself is not apples-to-apples. Assembling every installation, validation, and ownership cost element for each machine takes weeks and is often truncated under deadline pressure. Knowing which lifecycle cost elements to include requires cross-functional expertise that is rarely fully assembled for a comparison. Omitted validation, tooling, and ownership costs mean the cheapest-looking machine is frequently not the cheapest to own.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in quotation exclusions and pricing terms
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguity in quotation exclusions and pricing terms is a procurement/commercial matter with negligible bearing on the cartoning function (very low relevance), risking only cost disputes (low severity). Held below 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: Quotation terms are habitually vague and non-standard, so ambiguity in exclusions and pricing persists across most quotes. Clarifying ambiguous exclusions and pricing terms requires repeated supplier queries that slow quote comparison. Interpreting quotation terms takes procurement and contracts expertise that many technical evaluators lack. Ambiguous exclusions hide costs that later emerge as change orders, undermining budget accuracy.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of quoted commercial terms that conflict with purchaser approval requirements
rows.imp_rat: Quoted commercial terms conflicting with approval requirements is a purely commercial-approval error with little bearing on the cartoning function and mild, correctable consequences. Ceiling applies.
rows.sat_rat: Standard approval matrices catch most conflicting commercial terms, though edge-case clauses occasionally pass unflagged. Quote-to-approval reviews are routine and reasonably timely within procurement cycles. Procurement staff are trained to screen terms against approval policy, reducing dependence on rare expertise. Non-conforming terms mainly cost negotiation time rather than direct money.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount by which the total acquisition cost exceeds the approved project budget
rows.imp_rat: Acquisition cost exceeding approved budget is a purely administrative procurement error, largely irrelevant to the machine functioning and with trivial operational consequences.
rows.sat_rat: Total-cost tracking is fragmented across procurement, engineering, and finance, so the final number frequently surprises the project. Budget-vs-actual reconciliation happens late in the acquisition timeline, so overruns surface only after commitments are locked. Reliable cost forecasting for complex cartoning lines demands experienced estimators who are scarce and inconsistently involved. Acquisition costs routinely creep past approved budgets because scope changes, currency, and integration extras are chronically underestimated with only spreadsheet controls.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency among stakeholder requirements for the cartoning machinery purchase
rows.imp_rat: Inconsistent stakeholder requirements is a requirements-gathering ambiguity peripheral to producing cartons, with low, correctable consequences. Ceiling applies.
rows.sat_rat: Stakeholder requirements are commonly inconsistent and captured informally, so conflicts persist into procurement. Aligning stakeholder requirements is an iterative process that extends the front end of acquisition. Facilitating consensus across production, quality, engineering, and finance demands strong requirements-management skill. Unreconciled requirements cause expensive late changes and scope disputes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount by which the purchase commitment exceeds the approved capital budget
rows.imp_rat: Purchase commitment exceeding the capital budget is a financial-governance error unrelated to physically running cartons (very low relevance) with budget-overrun consequences (low-moderate severity), held low.
rows.sat_rat: Budget gating and purchase approval workflows are mature and routinely catch overcommitment, making breaches relatively uncommon. Capital approval and budget-check gates are established steps that fit within the acquisition timeline. Comparing commitment against an approved budget is straightforward finance work well within team capability. Formal capital-authorization controls directly guard against committing beyond the approved budget.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved warranty start-date conflicts across purchase, acceptance, commissioning, and registration records
rows.imp_rat: Warranty start-date conflicts across records is a purely administrative reconciliation error irrelevant to machine function, with minor contractual consequences, capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Warranty start dates are captured in disconnected systems, so conflicting dates across purchase, acceptance, and registration are common and unresolved. Reconciling warranty start dates across records is a manual afterthought that lags commissioning by weeks or months. It takes contract-savvy staff to spot conflicting warranty triggers across purchase and acceptance documents. Ambiguous start dates can silently shorten coverage, exposing the buyer to avoidable repair costs.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of inconsistency among supplier definitions for cartoning machinery types and configurations
rows.imp_rat: Inconsistent supplier definitions for machine types is a terminology-alignment nuisance during selection, peripheral to the CFJ with trivial consequences. Held under 2.0.
rows.sat_rat: There is no universal taxonomy for cartoner types and configurations, so supplier definitions routinely conflict. Reconciling divergent supplier definitions of machine types adds back-and-forth clarification time. Normalizing inconsistent vendor terminology requires experienced engineers who know the equipment class. Definitional mismatches cause mis-scoped buys whose correction costs money.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of mismatch between the purchaser's requested demonstration scope and the supplier's understood scope
rows.imp_rat: Mismatch between requested and understood demonstration scope is a pre-purchase communication error, peripheral to the core job with mild consequences. Capped below prior batch.
rows.sat_rat: Written demo protocols reduce mismatch, yet requested versus understood scope still diverges on details. Demonstration scope alignment happens in pre-award meetings but is often finalized late. Clearly framing FAT/demo scope needs a knowledgeable buyer to prevent misunderstanding. Scope mismatches trigger repeat demonstrations or travel whose cost is moderate.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that available references do not represent comparable sterile fill-finish installations
rows.imp_rat: References not representing comparable installations is an evaluation-input error, remote from the cartoning function itself, with modest consequences. Ceiling enforced.
rows.sat_rat: Comparable sterile fill-finish references are scarce, so available references often do not represent the intended application. Finding truly comparable sterile fill-finish references takes extended vetting time. Judging whether a reference installation is genuinely comparable requires niche sterile-processing expertise. Site visits to verify comparability add travel and staff cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that site-route constraints block movement of the machinery to its intended position
rows.imp_rat: Site-route constraints blocking machinery movement (relevance ~1) is a logistics/installation planning error remote from the cartoning function itself; severity (~2) since it can be pre-checked and mitigated, causing at most reschedule delay.
rows.sat_rat: Pre-move route surveys make blockages uncommon, though unanticipated site constraints still occasionally arise. Route surveys are typically done ahead of delivery, giving time to plan movement. Rigging planners and site engineers reliably assess access routes when engaged. A blocked route forces costly rework or partial disassembly, but planning usually prevents it.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the configuration requirements provided to the operator
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguity in configuration requirements slows the operator but is an informational error with limited direct damage, so relevance 2 and severity 1 fit under the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Vague or conflicting config guidance regularly produces inconsistent setups. Chasing clarification on ambiguous requirements delays setup. Ambiguous configuration instructions force operators to rely on scarce interpretive expertise. Misinterpreted requirements cause rework whose cost outweighs any documentation savings.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of warehouse caser components being damaged while packaging is removed
rows.imp_rat: Damage while removing packaging is an unpacking-phase risk (low relevance) with some asset consequence (severity), well within the 2.0 ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Standard unpacking practice keeps most components intact, with occasional careless-blade damage. Unpacking is a short task and rarely a schedule bottleneck. Avoiding cuts, scrapes and gouges while stripping crating and film relies on careful manual technique. Minor unpacking scratches are usually cosmetic and low cost unless a sensor or surface is nicked.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of missing, incorrect, or mismatched components left undocumented during receiving inspection
rows.imp_rat: Undocumented mismatched components during receiving inspection is an administrative documentation gap far from the casing CFJ (very low relevance), though it can cause later confusion (low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Receiving logs are often incomplete, so mismatches go unrecorded and resurface downstream. Documenting discrepancies during receiving adds modest time to the intake process. Thorough receiving inspection requires attentive comparison against BOMs more than specialized skill. Undocumented shortages later cause claim disputes and unrecoverable costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of missing or incorrect supplied components and fasteners discovered during assembly
rows.imp_rat: Missing or incorrect supplied components discovered during assembly is an assembly/procurement error peripheral to operating the caser (low relevance) with minor, correctable impact (very low severity).
rows.sat_rat: Kits are frequently short or mismatched, and gaps are commonly found only during assembly. Discovering a missing fastener mid-assembly stalls work while replacements are sourced. Cross-checking packing lists against actual parts needs diligence rather than deep expertise. Missing components trigger expedited shipping and idle labor, a recurring cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of compressed-air contamination entering the caser through the utility connection
rows.imp_rat: Compressed-air contamination via utility connection is a peripheral utility-quality issue for casing; its consequences are limited and it sits well below higher-ranked items, so relevance is minimal and severity low.
rows.sat_rat: Standard inline filters and dryers reliably keep compressed-air contamination out. Air quality is set at hookup and needs no ongoing time investment. Installing filters and dryers is standard utility work needing modest skill. Filtration hardware is inexpensive relative to the components it protects.

rows.source_job: Connect caser to plant control system
rows.stmt: Reduce the time the caser connection is delayed while required network access and integration approvals are obtained
rows.imp_rat: Delays awaiting network access and integration approvals are administrative/peripheral to the casing job itself (low relevance), with modest schedule consequences (severity), constrained under the 2.0 cap.
rows.sat_rat: Approval processes are bureaucratic and poorly scheduled against install timelines, making this a chronic delay with weak workarounds. Waiting for network access and integration approvals routinely delays connection, often for days. The bottleneck is IT/security process rather than technical skill on the floor. Delays idle commissioning resources and push schedules, a notable cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of case or product misfeeds that interrupt the trial run
rows.imp_rat: Misfeeds interrupting a trial run are a trial-phase reliability issue, largely recoverable and not core to steady production, so relevance 2 and severity 1 under the cap.
rows.sat_rat: Trial runs commonly stutter with misfeeds until the machine is dialed in, then stabilize. Each misfeed halts the trial and consumes troubleshooting time. Clearing and preventing misfeeds requires an experienced operator tuning guides and timing. Misfeeds waste a few cases and products per event, a modest recurring cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of trial cases sealed outside release requirements
rows.imp_rat: Trial cases sealed outside release requirements is a trial-phase quality error with limited scope, so relevance 2 and severity 1 satisfy the cap.
rows.sat_rat: Seal quality varies during trials until parameters settle, then meets release criteria consistently. Rejected seals require re-running trials, adding time. Achieving in-spec seals relies on technician skill setting glue or tape systems. Off-spec sealed cases are discarded, wasting cases and adhesive.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percent of required settings, calibration values, utility connections, and initial format parameters omitted from the Setzpacker baseline record
rows.imp_rat: Omitted baseline-record settings for the Setzpacker cause downstream setup rework, but this is largely administrative to the core casing job, so relevance stays 2 and severity 1 under the cap.
rows.sat_rat: Baseline records are notoriously partial, with calibration and utility details commonly omitted. Compiling a complete baseline record is a lengthy commissioning task often deferred. Capturing calibration values and utility specifications demands the commissioning technician's expertise. An incomplete baseline forces re-diagnosis later, adding cost, though recording itself is cheap.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of caser feed oscillations after an operator adjustment
rows.imp_rat: Feed oscillations after an operator adjustment are a tuning nuisance rather than a job-breaking error, so relevance is capped at 2 and severity at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Manual step adjustments on open-loop feeds frequently overshoot and hunt, making post-adjustment instability a common and poorly controlled failure. Oscillations settle out over several cycles, extending the disturbed window after each adjustment. Damping feed oscillation cleanly after a tweak leans heavily on operator feel, since most casers lack self-tuning feed control. Oscillating feed causes minor product misfeeds and rework that carry a small material cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the source of a Case Packer stoppage
rows.imp_rat: Misidentifying a stoppage source is a diagnostic ambiguity, marginal to executing the load and capped by ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Diagnostics are largely experience-based, so misidentifying causes is a recurring problem. Diagnosing stoppage source costs troubleshooting time. Correctly identifying the true stoppage cause demands significant operator or technician expertise. Misdiagnosis wastes effort and causes repeat stoppages.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of Case Packer stoppages recurring from an unresolved cause after restart
rows.imp_rat: Recurring stoppages from unresolved causes affect uptime but are peripheral to the grouping/loading job definition; capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Without root-cause tools, stoppages recur from unresolved causes at a persistent rate. Recurring stoppages from unresolved causes repeatedly cost time. Preventing recurrence depends on operator's diagnostic skill. Repeat stoppages waste throughput.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of duplicate tally entries created for the same cartoned unit
rows.imp_rat: Duplicate tally entries for the same unit are a data-hygiene defect that doesn't affect actual grouping/loading (low relevance) and are easily deduplicated (low severity), and ranking caps it.
rows.sat_rat: Manual counting allows recurring duplicate entries with limited automated checks. Removing duplicate tally entries costs reconciliation time. Avoiding double-counting depends on operator diligence. Duplicate entries distort counts.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of completed case-packing events omitted from the applicable production record
rows.imp_rat: Completed events omitted from the record is a documentation-completeness defect, peripheral to physical loading and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Mandatory records capture most events, though occasional omissions persist. Recovering omitted events costs later time. Recording every event depends on operator discipline. Omitted events create compliance costs.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of entering case-packing activity in the production record for the wrong batch
rows.imp_rat: Entering activity against the wrong batch is a record-attribution defect, peripheral to grouping/loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Batch record structure reduces wrong-batch entries, but manual entry leaves recurring risk. Confirming the correct batch before entry adds brief time. Entering the right batch depends on operator care with records. Wrong-batch entries cause costly record corrections.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a correction obscuring the original entry, rationale, or performer attribution
rows.imp_rat: A correction obscuring the original entry is a record-integrity error peripheral to physically grouping cartons; low consequence per ranking.
rows.sat_rat: GMP correction rules (single line, initial, date) are well established, so obscuring originals is relatively uncommon. Proper correction practice adds negligible time. Correct single-line correction is a well-trained basic GMP behavior. Obscured corrections risk costly audit findings.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration between completion of case-packing activity and entry in the applicable production record
rows.imp_rat: Delay between activity and record entry is a documentation-timeliness issue, marginal to physically loading cases and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Entries are often batched later, so entry lag is a recurring practice gap. Delay between activity and record entry is the core issue and often lingers. Timely entry depends on operator discipline. Delayed entries risk data reconstruction cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of case-packing entries missing required batch, case, date, or performer details
rows.imp_rat: Entries missing batch/case/date details is a data-completeness defect, peripheral to executing the load and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Structured forms reduce missing fields, but incomplete entries still recur. Completing missing entry details later costs time. Capturing all required fields depends on operator thoroughness. Incomplete entries drive costly rework and audit findings.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of packing interruptions, rejects, or reconciliation discrepancies omitted from the applicable production record
rows.imp_rat: Omitting interruptions/rejects from the record is a record-completeness defect, peripheral to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Interruptions and rejects are frequently omitted from records, a persistent documentation gap. Reconstructing omitted interruptions and rejects costs time later. Capturing all events depends on operator memory and diligence. Omitted records cause reconciliation and compliance costs.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting a torn, crushed, punctured, or moisture-damaged shipping case
rows.imp_rat: Accepting damaged shipping cases directly defeats the tertiary-packaging job and risks product loss, but the batch ceiling forces relevance and severity to 1.
rows.sat_rat: Visual inspection catches obvious damage, but subtle moisture or puncture damage occasionally passes. Inspecting incoming cases for damage adds brief time. Detecting torn or damaged cases relies on operator visual inspection. Accepting damaged cases wastes material and risks product loss.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling exposed by an unaccounted-for batch-record gap during audit scrutiny
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed by a batch-record gap during audit is a personal reputational worry peripheral to grouping cartons; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of anxiety while awaiting clarification of an ambiguous batch-record entry
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety awaiting clarification of an ambiguous batch-record entry is an emotional/documentation concern remote from grouping cartons; minimal consequence.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of lingering doubt after signing the batch record and line-clearance checks as complete
rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt after signing records is a personal emotional concern remote from grouping cartons; trivial stakes under the ranking.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of anxiety spikes as ungrouped cartoned units accumulate at the Case Packer
rows.imp_rat: Operator anxiety as units accumulate is an experiential instability, marginal to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when the upstream carton cadence changes unexpectedly
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure when carton cadence changes is a personal emotional-regulation concern peripheral to actually loading cases; minor consequence under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of uncertainty about continuing packing or requesting intervention while carton flow changes
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about continuing packing vs. intervention is a decision-latency ambiguity, marginal to the loading CFJ and capped.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of uncertainty about whether the correct cartoned units entered a closed shipping case
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about correct units entering a closed case is a verification-confidence ambiguity, peripheral to executing the load and capped low.

rows.stmt: Diminish the degree of anxiety while awaiting clarification of a questionable shipping-case count
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety while awaiting a questionable shipping-case count clarification is a purely emotional/administrative state peripheral to physically grouping and loading cartons; consequences are minor discomfort, not job failure.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a Case Packer procedural deviation being formally attributed to the operator
rows.imp_rat: A procedural deviation formally attributed to the operator is a reputational/compliance concern peripheral to grouping cases; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with Quality reviewers when the operator's Case Packer batch record is challenged
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when the batch record is challenged is a reputational concern peripheral to grouping cartons; minimal stakes under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of supervisors bypassing the operator in Case Packer segregation or line-clearance decisions
rows.imp_rat: Supervisors bypassing the operator in clearance decisions is an authority concern peripheral to grouping cartons; held minimal by the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of exclusion from high-scrutiny Case Packer assignments because compliance discipline is doubted
rows.imp_rat: Exclusion from high-scrutiny assignments due to doubted discipline is a career/reputation concern distant from grouping cases; minimal stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a case mix-up, count discrepancy, or integrity defect being visibly attributed to the operator
rows.imp_rat: A mix-up or defect visibly attributed to the operator is a reputational concern peripheral to the loading job's execution; held minimal by the ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with the line supervisor and Quality after a case discrepancy
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost after a case discrepancy is a reputational concern peripheral to grouping cartons; minimal consequence per the ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of the operator's case-acceptance judgments being second-guessed because packing consistency is doubted
rows.imp_rat: Case-acceptance judgments second-guessed due to doubted consistency is an evaluation-reputation concern peripheral to the loading job; held minimal by ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being bypassed for high-trust packing assignments because of a defect-associated reputation
rows.imp_rat: Being bypassed for high-trust assignments due to reputation is a career/reputation concern peripheral to physical loading; held minimal by ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of attributing an upstream or downstream disturbance to the case packer
rows.imp_rat: Attributing an upstream/downstream disturbance to the case packer is a diagnostic ambiguity, marginal to the grouping/loading job and held low.
rows.sat_rat: Without integrated line diagnostics, disturbances are frequently misattributed to the case packer. Misattributing an upstream/downstream disturbance wastes diagnostic time. Correctly isolating the source requires broad line experience that is scarce. Misattribution costs mainly wasted effort and time.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that original case-packer fault evidence is altered before evaluation
rows.imp_rat: Fault evidence altered before evaluation is a records-integrity ambiguity, tangential to physical loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Operators commonly clear faults immediately to restart, so original evidence is frequently altered before evaluation. Preserving fault evidence before evaluation costs little time. Knowing to preserve evidence needs trained discipline. Altered evidence causes misdiagnosis rather than direct cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of affected cartoned units with uncertain identity or disposition during diagnosis
rows.imp_rat: Units with uncertain identity during diagnosis is a traceability-confidence issue, peripheral to grouping/loading and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Manual tracking leaves unit identity uncertain during diagnosis fairly often. Resolving uncertain unit identity during diagnosis takes time. Tracking affected units requires diligent traceability skill. Uncertain disposition drives extra scrap or holds.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of conforming product held or discarded because the affected scope remains unclear
rows.imp_rat: Conforming product held due to unclear scope is a disposition-waste issue, tangential to the loading job and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Unclear scope leads to conservative over-holding as the default. Determining affected scope delays release of held product. Bounding the scope needs experienced judgement. Over-broad holds and discards waste conforming product, a direct cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of packaging items from the preceding run remaining when the next format starts
rows.imp_rat: Leftover packaging items from a preceding run is a line-clearance issue only loosely tied to the loading CFJ; capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Line-clearance procedures address this reasonably but residual items still occur. Removing leftover packaging items is a quick clearance task. It is mostly diligence rather than skill. Leftover items risk mix-ups more than material cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of degraded case-handling components remaining undetected after maintenance
rows.imp_rat: Undetected degraded handling components after maintenance is a maintenance-verification reliability issue, peripheral to loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Without condition monitoring, degraded case-handling components frequently pass undetected. Detecting degraded components takes inspection time during maintenance. Recognizing early degradation needs experienced condition assessment. Undetected degraded parts cause later failures and scrap.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of foreign material or maintenance residue left in the case packer after maintenance
rows.imp_rat: Foreign material/residue after maintenance is a housekeeping error marginal to the loading job; capped by the ranking ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Post-maintenance cleaning checks are standard but residue is sometimes left behind. Cleaning maintenance residue adds a post-maintenance step. Residue removal is straightforward diligence. Residue can contaminate product, a quality cost risk.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of case-packer unavailability beyond the scheduled maintenance window
rows.imp_rat: Unavailability beyond scheduled maintenance halts case packing, but ranking caps this below prior batch; relevance to keeping the job running and severity of lost output both held at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Maintenance windows are frequently overrun due to unforeseen issues, making this only moderately controlled. Unavailability beyond the scheduled maintenance window is the overrun operators want eliminated. Staying within the window depends on skilled, well-planned maintenance. Overrun is lost production capacity with direct cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance records with missing or inaccurate task-completion or as-left-setting entries
rows.imp_rat: Maintenance records with missing as-left entries is a documentation defect, peripheral to physically loading cases and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Manual maintenance logging routinely produces missing or inaccurate as-left entries. Completing accurate maintenance records adds minor documentation time. Accurate entries require procedural diligence. Poor records cause traceability rework, not direct material cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of closure material applied beyond the approved case-sealing requirement
rows.imp_rat: Excess closure material beyond sealing spec is a minor material-waste issue, tangential to the loading job and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Sealing systems generally apply consistent amounts, keeping overuse modest. Excess sealing material application is not a time issue. Minimizing overuse needs modest setup skill. Excess closure material (tape/glue) is a direct consumable waste.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of usable case material discarded during post-run line clearance
rows.imp_rat: Usable case material discarded during clearance is a material-waste concern, peripheral to the loading job and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Usable material is often discarded by default rather than recovered, a persistent minor loss. Line clearance discard is a quick end-of-run activity. Little skill is needed to segregate usable material. Discarding usable case material during clearance is avoidable waste.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of carton or case debris remaining in the case packer when packing resumes
rows.imp_rat: Carton/case debris remaining when packing resumes is a cleanliness concern peripheral to grouping/loading; consequences modest and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Debris is usually cleared during clearance but hidden debris occasionally remains. Clearing debris before resume adds a short cleaning step. Debris removal needs little specialized skill. Debris causes jams rather than direct cost.

rows.source_job: Verify pack quality before run release
rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of startup cases selected for verification that do not represent sustained packing conditions
rows.imp_rat: Startup cases not representing sustained conditions is a validation-sampling reliability concern, marginal to the core loading job and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Startup sample selection is loosely defined, so non-representative cases are commonly verified. Selecting representative startup cases is quick but often arbitrary. Judging which cases represent sustained conditions needs process understanding. Unrepresentative samples pass bad conditions, causing later waste.

rows.source_job: Verify pack quality before run release
rows.stmt: Reduce the time from production of the first packed case to completion of pack-quality verification
rows.imp_rat: Time to complete pack-quality verification is a verification-cycle delay, not central to executing the load itself; capped by ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Manual verification is reasonably repeatable but not fast. Time from first packed case to completed pack-quality verification is a direct startup delay. Faster verification depends on skilled inspection. Delay extends the scrap-generating startup window.

rows.source_job: Verify pack quality before run release
rows.stmt: Reduce the number of pack-quality verification records associated with the wrong batch or run
rows.imp_rat: Verification records tied to the wrong batch is a records-mapping defect, tangential to physical case packing and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Manual batch record entry produces occasional wrong-run associations. Correctly associating records takes little time when done at the station. Accurate record linking needs procedural diligence. Misfiled records cause traceability rework, not direct material cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when an unexpected case-packer indication suggests a deviation
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure when an indication suggests a deviation is a personal emotional-regulation concern remote from physical loading; trivial stakes per ranking.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of visible uncertainty about safeguard readiness at the case-packing go/no-go point
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about safeguard readiness at go/no-go is a decision-clarity ambiguity, marginal to executing the load and capped.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of self-doubt after changing a case-packer setup or adjustment
rows.imp_rat: Self-doubt after changing a setup is a personal emotional state, not central to the loading job; consequences trivial under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of visible uncertainty about the go/no-go decision while the team awaits run release
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about the go/no-go decision awaiting release is a decision-latency ambiguity, marginal to the loading CFJ and capped.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when initial carton grouping or case clearance differs from expectations
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure when clearance differs from expectations is a personal emotional state, not central to loading; trivial consequence per ranking.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of doubt triggered by conflicting format, grouping, or clearance indications during first-run release
rows.imp_rat: Doubt from conflicting first-run indications is a release-timing ambiguity peripheral to the physical loading job; trivial stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of losing composure during abrupt packing-demand changes while the sterile line is running
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure during abrupt demand changes is a personal emotional-regulation concern remote from the loading job; trivial stakes under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of visible uncertainty about the next case-packing decision while others await direction
rows.imp_rat: Visible uncertainty about the next decision while others await direction is a coordination-timing worry, not central to the loading job; consequences trivial.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of second-guessing a packing decision when case-packer behavior conflicts with upstream cues
rows.imp_rat: Second-guessing a packing decision when cues conflict is a subjective decision-confidence state, not central to loading; low consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a formal packaging deviation is attributed to the operator's case-packer setup decisions
rows.imp_rat: A deviation attributed to the operator's setup decisions is a reputational/compliance concern distant from physical loading; minimal stakes under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with quality after a case-content discrepancy is linked to the operator's work
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost after a discrepancy linked to the operator is a reputational concern distant from grouping cartons; minimal consequence under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which the operator's case-packing decisions are second-guessed during batch review
rows.imp_rat: Packing decisions second-guessed during batch review is an evaluation-reputation concern distant from physical loading; minimal stakes under the ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a sterile-line stoppage is publicly attributed to the operator's case-packer setup or adjustment
rows.imp_rat: A sterile-line stoppage attributed to the operator's setup is a reputational concern distant from physically loading cases; minimal stakes under the ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with the fill-finish line team after visible intervention at the operator's station
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost after visible intervention at the operator's station is a reputational concern peripheral to physical loading; low consequence under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of public second-guessing of the operator's readiness calls by the line team or shift leadership
rows.imp_rat: Public second-guessing of readiness calls is an interpersonal-reputation concern distant from physical loading; minimal consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage difference between forecast and actual shift packing workload
rows.imp_rat: Forecast-vs-actual workload difference is a planning-accuracy concern, peripheral to the grouping/loading job and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Workload forecasts are approximate and routinely diverge from actual shift demand. Forecast error is only revealed as the shift unfolds, too late to fully adjust. Better forecasting needs planning analytics skill not always present at line level. Forecast misses drive over- or under-staffing, directly wasting labor.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of excess operator labor assigned after packing workload declines
rows.imp_rat: Excess labor assigned after workload declines is a labor-waste issue, marginal to executing the load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Reactive downsizing means excess labor persists inconsistently after declines. Standing down excess labor after a decline lags the workload drop. Reallocating idle staff needs supervisory judgement but little technical skill. Excess labor held after demand falls is pure wasted spend, the core loss here.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that staffing decisions use conflicting workload and operator-availability data
rows.imp_rat: Staffing decisions on conflicting data is a workforce-planning ambiguity, tangential to physically loading cases and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Workload and availability data often live in separate spreadsheets and systems, so conflicting inputs are common. Reconciling conflicting data before deciding costs planning time. Interpreting conflicting sources takes supervisory judgement. Bad staffing decisions waste labor or lose output.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that personnel with required authority or expertise are unavailable when escalation is needed
rows.imp_rat: Unavailable escalation authority is a resource-availability reliability concern, peripheral to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: On-call and shift-leader coverage help but off-hours gaps make availability inconsistent. Waiting for the right authority to become available adds escalation delay. This need is about access to scarce expertise/authority when needed, the defining skill constraint. Unavailable authority prolongs downtime, an indirect cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time from disruption recognition to notification of all accountable functions
rows.imp_rat: Time to notify functions of a disruption is a communication-latency delay, peripheral to grouping/loading and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Ad-hoc notification means some functions are informed late or missed, so it is inconsistent. Notifying all accountable functions relies on manual calls/radio, introducing delay. Knowing whom to notify takes situational familiarity but little technical skill. Notification delay extends downtime cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of disagreement among accountable functions about disruption status and restart conditions
rows.imp_rat: Disagreement about disruption/restart conditions is a coordination ambiguity, marginal to the grouping/loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Without a shared status view, functions frequently diverge on disruption state and restart conditions. Disagreement over status prolongs decision time during disruptions. Aligning functions requires experienced coordination and authority. Misalignment delays restart, wasting capacity.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that case-packing output is misjudged because shift KPI data are incomplete or conflicting
rows.imp_rat: Output misjudged from incomplete KPI data is a reporting ambiguity, peripheral to the loading CFJ and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Shift KPI data are often fragmented across systems, so output is frequently misjudged from incomplete or conflicting figures. Assembling complete KPI data lags, so judgements are made on stale numbers. Interpreting incomplete or conflicting KPIs takes analytical experience. Misjudged output leads to wrong interventions that waste resources.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of shipping-case identifiers unreadable during traceability verification
rows.imp_rat: Unreadable case identifiers during traceability verification is a marking defect, marginal to physically loading cartons; capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Print-quality checks and scanners keep most codes readable, though smudged or damaged labels still occur. Unreadable IDs are found at verification, forcing time-consuming manual lookup. Reading damaged codes may need operator interpretation but is not highly skilled. Unreadable codes cause rework and occasional case loss.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of duplicate shipping-case identifiers within a batch record
rows.imp_rat: Duplicate shipping-case identifiers in a batch record is a labeling/data defect, peripheral to grouping and loading; capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Sequential serialization systems make duplicates rare where deployed. Duplicate IDs are caught at reconciliation, not at creation. Prevention relies on correct code-management setup rather than operator skill. Duplicates force rework of records, a modest cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that traceability records are altered without authorized attribution
rows.imp_rat: Traceability records altered without attribution is a records-integrity defect, peripheral to executing the load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Validated electronic records with audit trails and access control make unattributed alteration very rare where deployed. Audit-trail review to detect unauthorized changes happens after the fact. Maintaining controlled records requires trained compliance discipline. Unattributed alterations create compliance liability far exceeding control cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of unresolved traceability discrepancies before case disposition
rows.imp_rat: Duration of unresolved traceability discrepancies before disposition is a downstream administrative delay, peripheral to physically grouping/loading cartons and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Resolution timeliness is erratic and depends on investigator availability. Traceability discrepancies can sit unresolved for long stretches awaiting investigation. Resolution needs QA analytical judgement often not immediately available. Unresolved discrepancies hold WIP and consume investigation labor.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to retrieve complete traceability evidence for a questioned shipping case
rows.imp_rat: Time to retrieve traceability evidence for a questioned case is a retrieval-latency delay, tangential to grouping/loading and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Where electronic batch records exist retrieval is fairly dependable, but paper and mixed systems make completeness uncertain. Retrieving full traceability evidence for a questioned case can take hours of manual record-pulling. Finding and assembling records needs familiarity with the record systems. Slow retrieval extends holds and investigation labor.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when downstream shipping challenges projected case volume near shift end
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure at shift end is peripheral emotional strain with limited direct effect on grouping and loading cases; capped at 1 on both.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of uncertainty about whether case-packing output will satisfy downstream shipping before shift end
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about whether output satisfies downstream shipping before shift end is a forecasting-timing worry remote from loading; minimal stakes.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of doubt about the case-packing outlook when shift indicators conflict
rows.imp_rat: Doubt about the outlook when shift indicators conflict is a subjective forecasting worry, not central to loading cases; low stakes under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of feeling exposed when asked to explain the case-output outlook without a defensible answer
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed when explaining the outlook without an answer is a personal emotional concern peripheral to the loading job; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of uncertainty while tracing a questioned shipping case for an auditor
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty while tracing a questioned case for an auditor is a documentation-timing concern distant from the loading job; low consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when inconsistent case records are challenged
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure when case records are challenged is an interpersonal error peripheral to case-packing execution; held at 1 on both.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of confidence lost when case-record evidence conflicts
rows.imp_rat: Confidence lost when case-record evidence conflicts is a subjective/documentation concern remote from physical loading; trivial consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of doubt after evidence for shipping-case grouping and loading is reviewed
rows.imp_rat: Doubt after grouping/loading evidence is reviewed is a review-confidence worry peripheral to the physical loading job; minimal consequence.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when the team seeks direction during a case-packer stoppage
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure when the team seeks direction during a stoppage is a leadership-emotion error peripheral to the packing job; held at 1 on both.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of uncertainty about the next quality-preserving direction during a live stoppage
rows.imp_rat: Duration of uncertainty about the next direction during a stoppage is a subjective coordination delay, peripheral to the core loading job; the ranking already placed it below batch 3, capping it at trivial stakes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of anxiety spikes while trustworthy stoppage status is unavailable
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety spikes while stoppage status is unavailable is an emotional/coordination worry remote from the loading job; trivial stakes per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of doubt about the recovery decision when production and quality signals conflict
rows.imp_rat: Doubt about the recovery decision when signals conflict is a decision-timing concern remote from the physical packing job; low stakes per the ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a regulatory finding is publicly attributed to Case Packer governance before inspectors and site leadership
rows.imp_rat: A regulatory finding publicly attributed to governance is a reputational concern remote from physically loading cartons; low stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of credibility lost with quality and batch-release stakeholders when Case Packer traceability is challenged
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when traceability is challenged is a reputational concern distant from physically loading cases; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which the overseer is bypassed in Case Packer batch-disposition decisions because governance is not trusted
rows.imp_rat: Overseer bypassed in batch-disposition due to distrust is a governance error, indirect to the CFJ; both proxies capped at 1.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a public commitment to continuous Case Packer inspection readiness is visibly missed before site leadership or regulatory stakeholders
rows.imp_rat: A public inspection-readiness commitment visibly missed is a reputational concern remote from grouping cartons; low stakes per the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that plant leadership publicly attributes a scheduled Case Packer output miss to the overseer's governance
rows.imp_rat: Public attribution of an output miss to the overseer is a reputational concern remote from grouping cartons; low stakes under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with plant leadership when reported Case Packer output conflicts with completed-case results
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when reported output conflicts with results is a reporting-reputation concern distant from physical loading; low consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of being bypassed by plant leadership in Case Packer commitment decisions
rows.imp_rat: Being bypassed in commitment decisions is a governance error with indirect job link; ceiling forces relevance and severity to 1.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of being bypassed by plant leadership in Case Packer recovery decisions
rows.imp_rat: Being bypassed in recovery decisions is a governance error with indirect effect on running the line; both proxies held at 1.
