rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required case-packing validation records that are missing, inaccurate, or not attributable
rows.imp_rat: Missing/inaccurate validation records is a documentation-attribution defect, peripheral to physical case packing and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Validation documentation controls exist but attributability and accuracy gaps persist. Correcting validation records adds rework delay. Attributable, accurate validation records need specialist rigor. Missing validation records risk regulatory cost and requalification.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration that required case-packing validation review or requalification remains overdue
rows.imp_rat: Overdue validation review/requalification is a schedule-compliance delay, peripheral to grouping/loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Scheduling exists but validation reviews commonly slip beyond due dates. Overdue validation review and requalification durations frequently run past due. Timely completion depends on scarce validation SME capacity. Overdue requalification risks holds and compliance exposure cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time from emergence of an adverse case-packing trend to recognition that validation assumptions may no longer hold
rows.imp_rat: Time to recognize a trend invalidates assumptions is a monitoring-latency delay, tangential to executing the load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Trending is often periodic and manual, so validation-relevant signals are recognized slowly and inconsistently. Lag from adverse trend emergence to recognizing validation risk is typically long. Recognition depends on scarce analytical and trending expertise. Late recognition allows accumulating scrap and rework costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of case-packing assignments performed by personnel lacking current qualification
rows.imp_rat: Assignments by unqualified personnel is a staffing-compliance issue, tangential to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Skills-matrix scheduling reliably restricts assignments, with rare cover-shift lapses. Assignment checks against qualification are quick. Assigning only qualified staff relies on supervisor discipline. Unqualified assignments risk error-driven rework.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration that packed lots remain on quality hold while required release evidence is unavailable
rows.imp_rat: Lots on quality hold awaiting release evidence is a release-delay concern, peripheral to executing the load and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Evidence-gathering practices exist but availability gaps regularly prolong holds. Lots wait on quality hold when release evidence is unavailable, extending duration. Speed depends on reviewers knowing where to find evidence. Prolonged holds carry inventory and expedite costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of assigned case-packing personnel whose qualification evidence is missing or expired
rows.imp_rat: Missing/expired personnel qualification evidence is an administrative gap, peripheral to physically packing cases and capped.
rows.sat_rat: LMS training matrices keep most qualifications current, though lapses occur at expiry. Qualification tracking is administrative, not a bottleneck. Managing qualification evidence relies on training-system administration. Expired qualifications risk rework and compliance cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of case-packing equipment items with undocumented or overdue calibration, maintenance, or qualification status
rows.imp_rat: Overdue calibration/maintenance status is an equipment-governance defect, marginal to executing the load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: CMMS-driven scheduling keeps most equipment current, with occasional overdue items. Calibration/maintenance scheduling is routine, not a delay. Status tracking relies on planning systems more than individual skill. Overdue equipment risks nonconforming output and rework.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of case-packing deviation or corrective-action records remaining open beyond approved due dates
rows.imp_rat: Deviation records open beyond due dates is a closure-timeliness delay, peripheral to loading cartons and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Tracking systems flag overdue items but backlog persistence is common. CAPA and deviation records routinely remain open past due dates. On-time closure depends on owner discipline and workload management. Aging open records tie up resources and holds.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to retrieve requested case-packing evidence during an audit
rows.imp_rat: Time to retrieve evidence during audit is a records-retrieval delay, peripheral to physically loading cases and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Document systems help, but mixed paper/electronic records make retrieval time variable. Audit evidence retrieval can be slow when records are dispersed across systems and paper. Fast retrieval depends on staff knowing where evidence resides. Slow retrieval extends audits and consumes staff time.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of case-packing quality events omitted from or misclassified in unit KPI data
rows.imp_rat: Quality events omitted from KPI data is a reporting-accuracy defect, marginal to grouping/loading and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: KPI capture is often manual and inconsistent, so events are omitted or misclassified fairly frequently. KPI classification is periodic reporting work, not a delay. Correct classification depends on analyst judgement and definitions. Misclassified events distort resource decisions and hide costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of deviation investigations missing required case-packing evidence
rows.imp_rat: Deviation investigations missing evidence is an investigation-completeness defect, peripheral to physical loading and held low.
rows.sat_rat: Investigation templates prompt evidence capture, but required case-packing evidence is often incomplete. Retrieving missing investigation evidence delays closure. Knowing what evidence is required depends on investigator expertise. Missing evidence prolongs investigations and holds.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time from deviation detection to initiation of containment and investigation
rows.imp_rat: Time from detection to containment initiation is a response-latency delay, peripheral to the grouping/loading job and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Escalation procedures exist but timeliness varies widely across shifts. Delay between detection and containment initiation is a persistent lag. Prompt initiation depends on operator recognition and escalation training. Slow containment widens exposure and inventory cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of closed deviations lacking a substantiated root cause
rows.imp_rat: Closed deviations lacking substantiated root cause is a quality-process defect, tangential to loading cartons and capped.
rows.sat_rat: RCA tools exist but many closures rest on weak or assumed root causes. Root-cause work extends deviation closure time. Substantiated root cause depends on scarce structured-investigation skill. Unsubstantiated closures allow recurrence and repeated costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration that affected product remains on hold awaiting disposition
rows.imp_rat: Product on hold awaiting disposition is a release-delay concern, peripheral to grouping/loading and capped by ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Disposition workflows exist but timeliness is inconsistent and frequently slow. Hold duration awaiting disposition is a chronic, often extended lag. Faster disposition depends on scarce QA reviewer availability. Extended holds tie up inventory and working capital.

rows.stmt: Reduce the rate of recurrence of the same case-packing deviation after closure
rows.imp_rat: Recurrence of the same deviation after closure is a corrective-action effectiveness issue, tangential to loading cartons and capped.
rows.sat_rat: CAPA processes exist but the same case-packing deviations recur, indicating weak effectiveness verification. Recurrence forces repeat investigation time. Preventing recurrence depends on effective CAPA design skill. Repeat deviations multiply rework and investigation cost.

rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about whether records withstand auditor scrutiny is a documentation-confidence concern peripheral to physical loading; trivial consequence per ordering.

rows.imp_rat: Doubt from conflicting clearance/traceability records is a record-ambiguity concern peripheral to the loading job; low stakes under the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when an auditor challenges a case-packing record discrepancy
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure under auditor challenge is interpersonal strain with weak direct bearing on grouping/loading cases; ceiling caps both at 1.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when stakeholders press for a case-packing deviation decision
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure under pressure for a deviation decision is emotional strain peripheral to case loading; capped at 1 on both.

rows.imp_rat: Visible uncertainty about lot disposition while awaiting direction is a communication-timing concern, not central to loading cases; consequences are limited to short-lived ambiguity.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of anxiety spikes while awaiting facts needed to assess a case-packing deviation
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety spikes awaiting deviation facts are emotional strain with weak link to the core job; capped at 1 for relevance and severity.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of confidence erosion under conflicting quality and production interpretations of a case-packing deviation
rows.imp_rat: Confidence erosion under conflicting deviation interpretations is a subjective/coordination concern peripheral to the loading job; trivial consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of anxiety while awaiting evidence needed to judge sealed-case shipping integrity
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety awaiting sealed-case integrity evidence is an emotional/coordination worry remote from physical loading; trivial consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of losing calm when case-packing integrity indicators conflict
rows.imp_rat: Losing calm when integrity indicators conflict is a personal emotional-regulation concern peripheral to grouping cases; low consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing composure when challenged to justify the integrity of released cases
rows.imp_rat: Losing composure defending released-case integrity is interpersonal strain peripheral to the CFJ; both proxies capped at 1.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that quality leadership or auditors publicly attribute a critical compliance observation to the overseer's case-packer governance
rows.imp_rat: Public attribution of a compliance observation to the overseer's governance is a reputational concern peripheral to grouping cartons; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of credibility lost with auditors when the overseer cannot substantiate case-packer inspection readiness
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when inspection readiness cannot be substantiated is a reputational concern peripheral to physically loading cases; trivial stakes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of case-packer compliance decisions in which quality leadership bypasses the overseer due to doubts about the overseer's judgment
rows.imp_rat: Compliance decisions bypassing the overseer due to judgment doubts is an authority/reputation matter peripheral to physical packing; minor consequence.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which quality leadership publicly discredits the overseer's case-packer inspection-readiness commitments
rows.imp_rat: Quality publicly discrediting inspection-readiness commitments is a reputational concern peripheral to physical loading; trivial consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that quality or site leadership publicly attributes an escaped nonconforming case to the overseer's governance
rows.imp_rat: Public attribution of an escaped nonconforming case to the overseer is a reputational concern distant from grouping cartons; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of credibility lost with quality or regulatory stakeholders when case-packing KPI assurances conflict with review findings
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when KPI assurances conflict with findings is a reporting-reputation concern peripheral to the loading job; low stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which quality or site leadership bypasses the overseer in shipping-integrity escalation or disposition decisions
rows.imp_rat: Leadership bypassing the overseer in escalation is an organizational-authority matter peripheral to grouping cartons; consequences limited to overseer standing.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a missed shipping-integrity commitment causes public loss of trust in the overseer among site leaders
rows.imp_rat: Public loss of trust from a missed commitment is a reputational/interpersonal concern remote from grouping cases; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of assigning an incorrect root cause to a case-loading failure
rows.imp_rat: Assigning an incorrect root cause is an investigation-accuracy defect, peripheral to the grouping/loading job and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Root-cause tools exist but for case-loading failures misattribution is common due to weak data. Incorrect root cause prolongs investigation and repeat failures over time. Accurate root-cause attribution depends heavily on scarce, experienced investigators. Wrong root causes drive ineffective fixes and recurring failure costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percent of case-loading failures lacking complete carton, grouping, case, and line-condition records
rows.imp_rat: Failures lacking complete condition records is a records-completeness ambiguity, peripheral to physical loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Records of case-loading failures are frequently incomplete, so root-cause data is often lacking. Reconstructing missing failure records extends investigation time. Capturing complete carton/line-condition records requires disciplined data practice. Missing records prolong investigations and repeat failures cost money.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of case-loading failures that cannot be reproduced under their original production conditions
rows.imp_rat: Non-reproducible loading failures is a diagnostic-reliability issue, tangential to the grouping/loading job and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Incomplete condition capture makes many case-loading failures non-reproducible, a persistent weakness. Reproducing failures under original conditions consumes investigation time. Recreating production conditions demands strong troubleshooting and data-analysis skill. Non-reproducible failures prolong investigations and defer fixes at cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that physical evidence from failed cartons or cases is altered before examination
rows.imp_rat: Physical failure evidence altered before examination is a records-integrity defect, peripheral to grouping/loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Formal evidence-preservation for case-loading failures is inconsistent, so physical evidence is frequently disturbed before review. Preserving evidence quickly requires prompt quarantine, a time-sensitive step. Proper evidence preservation depends on procedural discipline more than deep skill. Altered evidence causes misdiagnosis and repeat-failure costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time between a case-loading failure and examination of the affected cartons and case
rows.imp_rat: Time to examine failed cartons/case is an investigation-latency delay, peripheral to loading cartons and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Ad hoc response means examination is often delayed, degrading evidence and consistency. The gap between a failure and examining affected cartons is directly a response-time problem. Prompt examination requires available trained investigators and procedures. Delays let failures recur, adding scrap cost before containment.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of unresolved conflict among production, quality, and engineering findings about a loading failure
rows.imp_rat: Unresolved conflict among findings about a failure is a resolution-latency delay, marginal to the loading CFJ and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: CAPA governance eventually resolves conflicts, but production/quality/engineering disagreements often linger unresolved. Unresolved cross-functional disputes directly extend time to closure. Resolving conflicting findings needs facilitation and technical arbitration skill. Prolonged disputes delay corrective action and let costs accumulate.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between approved configuration records and issued packaging specifications
rows.imp_rat: Discrepancies between config records and issued specs is a document-control error, tangential to loading cartons and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Document-control systems reduce discrepancies well, though issuance errors still occasionally slip through. Reconciling records to issued specs adds document-control time. Detecting record-to-spec discrepancies requires document-management diligence. Discrepancies cause nonconformances and rework costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of packaging configuration noncompliance after an applicable regulatory change
rows.imp_rat: Config noncompliance after regulatory change is a compliance-timeliness delay, tangential to physically loading cases and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Change-management processes eventually restore compliance, but lag between rule change and updated configuration is common. The duration of noncompliance after a regulatory change is a direct time-to-remediate problem. Rapid remediation needs regulatory and change-control expertise. Prolonged noncompliance risks fines and lost market access costs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of configuration-specific components and settings changed during a format switch
rows.imp_rat: Number of components changed during format switch is a changeover-complexity concern, peripheral to physically loading cases and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: SMED and modular tooling reduce changed items, but complex configs still require many component swaps. Changing many configuration-specific components lengthens changeover time. Executing multi-part changeovers requires trained setup technicians. More changed parts means more spares inventory and labor cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of production interruption caused by switching case-loading configurations
rows.imp_rat: Production interruption from switching configs is a changeover-downtime concern, peripheral to the physical loading job and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Changeover procedures and quick-change parts limit downtime, but switches still interrupt production meaningfully. Configuration-switch downtime is a direct, recurring production-time loss. Fast switchovers depend on trained crews and good procedures. Downtime during switches directly raises cost per unit.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of configuration-specific qualification work created by the case-loading specification
rows.imp_rat: Config-specific qualification workload created by the spec is an efficiency-waste concern, marginal to the physical load and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Qualification approaches are established, but the specification often drives more bespoke qualification than necessary. Configuration-specific qualification work extends project timelines. Scoping and executing qualification requires validation expertise. Each configuration-specific qualification consumes significant engineering and testing resources, a major recurring cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to verify grouping, orientation, and case-loading settings before production restart
rows.imp_rat: Time to verify settings before restart is a restart-latency delay, marginal to executing the load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Setup sheets and first-article checks make verification fairly consistent, though still partly manual and slow. Verifying settings before restart adds delay to production resumption. Correct setting verification requires setup and QA competence. Verification time is lost production, though it prevents costlier errors.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that shipping-case materials or loading activity introduce contaminants into controlled manufacturing areas
rows.imp_rat: Case materials introducing contaminants into controlled areas has some environmental stakes but is peripheral to the loading CFJ and capped by ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Segregation and material controls make case-material contamination of controlled areas rare where properly designed. Contamination controls add cleaning and gowning time around loading areas. Managing corrugate/materials contamination risk needs cleanroom and containment expertise. Contamination excursions cause costly scrap and remediation.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variation in evaluator interpretations of packaging criteria
rows.imp_rat: Variation in evaluator interpretations of criteria is an assessment-consistency issue, peripheral to loading cartons and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Training and defined criteria reduce spread, but subjective packaging criteria still yield inconsistent evaluations. Reconciling differing evaluator interpretations adds review cycles. Variation in interpretation stems directly from reliance on individual evaluator judgement and training. Divergent interpretations cause rework and inconsistent decisions with cost impact.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicts between standardized packaging criteria and sterile fill-finish quality or regulatory requirements
rows.imp_rat: Conflicts between packaging criteria and sterile requirements is a cross-standard reconciliation issue, peripheral to the physical load and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Cross-functional review catches many conflicts, but standardization pressure still collides with sterile requirements at times. Resolving conflicts between standardized criteria and sterile requirements takes cross-functional time. Reconciling packaging standardization with sterile fill-finish rules demands specialized regulated-product expertise. Unresolved conflicts cause deviations and costly compliance gaps.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration that approved evaluation criteria remain outdated after packaging or compliance requirements change
rows.imp_rat: Outdated evaluation criteria after requirement change is a document-currency delay, marginal to grouping/loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Periodic review updates criteria, but they routinely lag behind changed compliance requirements. Outdated criteria persisting after requirement changes is fundamentally a delay problem. Timely updates require regulatory monitoring and specification competence. Stale criteria produce defective decisions with rework cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that rare case-loading failures remain undetected before configuration approval
rows.imp_rat: Rare loading failures undetected before approval is a validation-coverage reliability concern, tangential to the CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Sample-based validation inherently under-detects low-frequency failures, so rare defects routinely escape approval. Detecting rare failures before approval requires extended or accelerated testing time. Designing trials to surface rare failures needs advanced statistical and reliability expertise. Undetected rare failures cause expensive field escapes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the rate of equivalent validation trials yielding conflicting pass-or-fail conclusions
rows.imp_rat: Equivalent trials yielding conflicting conclusions is a validation-consistency issue, peripheral to grouping/loading and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Without objective thresholds, equivalent trials still yield conflicting pass/fail outcomes fairly often. Resolving conflicting trial conclusions consumes extra review time. Reconciling equivalent trials needs statistical and methodological expertise. Conflicting conclusions cause repeat trials and delayed decisions with cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of validation trials with incomplete, incorrect, or untraceable records
rows.imp_rat: Validation trials with untraceable records is a documentation defect, marginal to the loading CFJ and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Validation records are variable in quality and incomplete or untraceable records remain common. Poor validation records force re-execution, wasting time. Complete, traceable trial records require good documentation discipline. Untraceable trials trigger repeat validation costs.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of doubt that signed case-loading limits can be defended from the evaluation record
rows.imp_rat: Doubt that signed limits are defensible is an evidence-confidence concern, tangential to grouping/loading and capped.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of uncertainty when auditors challenge the rationale for carton grouping and loading criteria
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty when auditors challenge grouping criteria is a review-timing concern remote from physical loading; held minimal by ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling exposed when an auditor challenges an assumption beyond evaluated packing conditions
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed when an auditor challenges an assumption is a personal reputational worry remote from grouping cases; trivial stakes per ranking.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of uncertainty about whether specified case loads will arrive intact and correctly counted
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about intact/correctly-counted loads is a downstream-confidence concern, marginal to the loading CFJ and capped low.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of anxiety episodes while awaiting downstream confirmation of case condition and carton counts
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety awaiting downstream confirmation of case condition is an emotional/coordination worry remote from grouping cartons; trivial stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling exposed without a defensible explanation when downstream damage or miscounts are reported
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed without a defensible explanation for reported damage is a personal reputational concern peripheral to grouping cases; low consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of uncertainty about the case-packer go-or-no-go recommendation while stakeholders await direction
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about the go/no-go recommendation while awaiting direction is a decision-timing concern remote from loading cartons; trivial stakes per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of anxiety episodes while awaiting evidence about case-loading readiness
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety awaiting case-loading readiness evidence is an emotional/coordination worry remote from physical loading; trivial consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of lingering doubt after reviewing evidence about hidden case-loading risks
rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt about hidden risks after review is an assurance-confidence issue, peripheral to executing the load and held at the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with regulatory reviewers when configuration rationale cannot be traced to supporting evidence
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost with regulators over untraceable rationale is a reputational/compliance concern peripheral to physically packing cases; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which quality reviewers second-guess recommendations because evaluation criteria appear inconsistently applied
rows.imp_rat: Reviewers second-guessing due to inconsistent criteria is an evaluation-reputation concern remote from loading cartons; low consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the specifier is bypassed in future case-packing decisions after an audited configuration is challenged
rows.imp_rat: Being bypassed after an audited configuration is challenged is an authority/reputation concern remote from grouping cartons; trivial stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with quality and validation leaders when the case-packing specification rationale fails review
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when specification rationale fails review is a reputational concern remote from physical loading; held minimal by the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which packaging engineering and site operations second-guess case-configuration recommendations during approval
rows.imp_rat: Engineering second-guessing configuration recommendations during approval is an authority/reputation concern remote from grouping cases; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an assurance of intact cartons, correct case counts, and stable loads is remembered as a missed commitment
rows.imp_rat: An assurance remembered as a missed commitment is a reputational/interpersonal concern remote from grouping units into cases; minor stakes under the ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of relevant product, packaging, shift, changeover, and environmental variables omitted from the risk assessment
rows.imp_rat: Variables omitted from the risk assessment is an analysis-completeness error upstream of loading; consequences minor under the batch ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Risk-assessment templates help but relevant variables are still frequently omitted. Including all variables in the risk assessment adds planning time. Identifying all relevant product, shift, and environmental variables requires broad expertise. Omitted variables cause costly latent failures.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of disagreement among quality, engineering, production, and supplier evidence about case-packing risk
rows.imp_rat: Disagreement among functions about packing risk is a governance-alignment ambiguity, distant from the physical load and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Risk-review forums exist but genuine consensus on case-packing risk is inconsistently achieved. Resolving cross-functional disagreement on risk lengthens assessment cycles. Harmonizing quality, engineering, production, and supplier views requires senior facilitation skill. Disagreement drives repeated meetings and rework rather than material cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of risk-assessment delays caused by unavailable approved specifications, packaging samples, or equipment records
rows.imp_rat: Risk-assessment delays from unavailable specs is a scheduling constraint, peripheral to the loading job and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Document systems mitigate this, but missing approved specs still stall risk assessments periodically. The loss is delay caused by unavailable specs, samples, or equipment records for risk assessment. Assembling records requires familiarity rather than deep skill. Delays tie up assessment resources at modest cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of critical grouping, count, orientation, and loading failures without assessed detection or rejection controls
rows.imp_rat: Critical failures without assessed detection controls is a control-coverage reliability gap, tangential to grouping/loading and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Control assessment exists but coverage of all critical failure types remains incomplete. Assessing detection controls for critical failures adds analysis time. Determining adequacy of detection/rejection controls requires strong engineering judgement. Uncontrolled critical failures cause costly escapes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of approved case-packing requirements without a corresponding protocol test
rows.imp_rat: Requirements without a corresponding protocol test is a coverage defect, peripheral to physically loading cases and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Traceability matrices are standard practice, so most requirements map to tests with occasional gaps. Building requirement-to-test coverage is upfront traceability work. Constructing a complete requirements trace matrix requires validation skill. Untested requirements surface as costly gaps later.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of intended operating limits and challenge conditions omitted from the case-packing protocol
rows.imp_rat: Operating limits omitted from protocol is a test-design defect, tangential to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Protocol templates help, but intended limits and challenges are still sometimes left out. Capturing all operating limits and challenges in the protocol is upfront authoring time. Knowing which limits and challenges to include requires strong process expertise. Omitted conditions drive later re-qualification cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which protocol configurations fail to represent specified carton formats, shipping-case formats, and loading patterns
rows.imp_rat: Protocol configs failing to represent formats is a validation-representativeness defect, tangential to executing the load and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Configuration matrices are used but frequently fail to fully represent all specified formats and patterns. Configuring protocols to represent all formats adds setup time but is manageable. Mapping protocol configurations to real formats and loading patterns needs format expertise. Unrepresentative configurations force costly re-runs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of critical miscount, damage, orientation, mix-up, and loading failure modes omitted from challenge tests
rows.imp_rat: Failure modes omitted from challenge tests is a test-coverage defect, marginal to executing the load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Challenge-test libraries exist but critical modes are still sometimes omitted. Including all critical failure modes in challenge tests adds test design time. Enumerating miscount, damage, orientation, and mix-up modes demands expert failure analysis. Omitted challenges surface as costly field failures.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variability in how test personnel interpret and execute case-packing protocol instructions
rows.imp_rat: Variability in interpreting protocol instructions is an execution-consistency issue, tangential to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Training and instructions reduce but do not eliminate interpretation variability across personnel. Inconsistent interpretation can slow execution when operators pause to clarify. Variability here is fundamentally about differing personnel interpretation, a classic skill-dependence gap. Misexecution wastes runs and materials.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of required observations, timestamps, settings, results, and exception details absent from protocol records
rows.imp_rat: Required observations absent from protocol records is a documentation-completeness defect, tangential to grouping/loading and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Structured forms and eBRs reduce missing fields, but gaps still appear in records. Ensuring all required fields are captured adds recording time. Complete data capture depends on execution discipline more than expertise. Missing records force costly re-runs or investigations.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in protocol rules for deviations, invalid runs, retesting, and acceptance disposition
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguity in deviation/retest/disposition rules is a protocol-clarity issue distant from the grouping/loading job; capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Deviation-handling SOPs exist but ambiguity in edge cases still forces inconsistent judgement calls. Deciding deviation and retest handling on the fly can stall runs when rules are unclear. Interpreting deviation, invalid-run, and disposition rules leans heavily on scarce quality judgement today. Ambiguous rules cause reruns and dispositions that waste resources.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of required material-status, line-clearance, personnel-readiness, calibration, and case-packer-readiness prerequisites omitted from the protocol
rows.imp_rat: Prerequisites omitted from the protocol is a protocol-completeness defect, peripheral to the loading CFJ and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Prerequisite checklists are standard but omissions still occur in protocols. Listing all prerequisites in the protocol is authoring overhead. Knowing the full prerequisite set needs experienced protocol authors. Missing prerequisites cause aborted runs and wasted resources.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of mismatch between documented qualification conditions and approved case-packing conditions
rows.imp_rat: Mismatch between qualified and approved conditions is a qualification-documentation defect, tangential to loading cartons and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Change-control links documents to approved conditions, but mismatches still slip through periodically. Reconciling documented versus approved conditions adds document-control time. Detecting mismatches requires careful documentation review competence. Mismatches trigger re-qualification costs when discovered late.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of approved qualification acceptance criteria lacking objective supporting evidence
rows.imp_rat: Acceptance criteria lacking objective evidence is a qualification-rigor defect, peripheral to physically loading cases and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Evidence-linking practice is established but some acceptance criteria still lack objective support. Providing objective evidence for each criterion adds documentation time. Determining what constitutes objective evidence needs validation judgement. Unsupported criteria cause costly inspection findings.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required cartoned-unit, shipping-case, and loading-pattern configurations omitted from qualification evidence
rows.imp_rat: Configurations omitted from qualification evidence is a documentation-coverage error peripheral to actually loading cases; minor stakes per ordering.
rows.sat_rat: Coverage matrices exist but required configurations are still sometimes omitted from evidence. Covering all configurations in qualification evidence extends test scope and time. Ensuring full configuration coverage needs format and pattern expertise. Missing configurations force later supplemental qualification cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of reported case-packing results that differ from attributable source observations
rows.imp_rat: Reported results differing from source observations is a data-integrity defect, marginal to executing the load and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Data-integrity controls and electronic capture make reported-versus-source mismatches increasingly rare. Verifying reported-versus-source results adds review time. Detecting transcription discrepancies needs careful reviewer attention. Discrepancies trigger costly data-integrity investigations.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of case-packing results not linked to the applicable acceptance criterion, tested configuration, equipment setting, and source record
rows.imp_rat: Results not linked to criteria/config/records is a traceability defect, peripheral to executing the load and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Data-integrity practices and eBRs improve linkage, though untraceable results still appear. Linking results to criteria and records is clerical overhead adding some time. Maintaining traceability links needs documentation discipline more than expertise. Unlinked results force costly re-investigation.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of qualification deviations lacking documented resolution, impact assessment, or approval
rows.imp_rat: Qualification deviations lacking resolution/approval is a documentation defect, peripheral to grouping/loading and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Deviation management systems ensure most deviations are documented, though some lack full resolution. Documenting deviation resolution and approval adds administrative time. Proper impact assessment and disposition require quality expertise. Open unresolved deviations tie up product at cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time from completion of case-packing qualification activities to approval of the evidence package
rows.imp_rat: Time from qualification completion to package approval is a process-cycle delay peripheral to the loading job; low stakes under the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Review workflows exist but approval cycle times remain chronically long. The core loss is the elapsed time from activity completion to evidence-package approval. Approval speed depends on reviewer availability rather than special skill. Delayed approval holds up qualified-state release at cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required packaging configurations, equipment settings, interventions, and challenge conditions omitted from qualification studies
rows.imp_rat: Conditions omitted from qualification studies is a documentation-coverage error peripheral to physical loading; held minimal by the ordering.
rows.sat_rat: Study-design practice covers most conditions but omissions of challenge conditions recur. Covering all settings, interventions, and challenges extends study duration. Identifying the full set of required conditions demands deep process knowledge. Omitted conditions drive costly re-studies.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of departures from the approved case-packing protocol during qualification execution
rows.imp_rat: Departures from protocol during qualification is an execution-compliance defect, marginal to executing the load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Execution oversight and review reduce departures, but they still occur during runs. Following the protocol precisely does not add time when execution is disciplined. Adhering to protocol depends on trained, attentive execution personnel. Protocol departures force costly re-execution.

rows.stmt: Reduce the rate of cartoned units miscounted, misoriented, or loaded incorrectly into shipping cases during qualification runs
rows.imp_rat: Miscounted or misloaded cartons in qualification runs relates directly to the loading job, but the batch ordering caps its importance at minimal despite its intrinsic relevance.
rows.sat_rat: Vision and count systems make miscount/misorientation during runs relatively well controlled. Miscount and misorientation errors are detected inline without added time. Automated verification reduces reliance on operator skill for count and orientation. Incorrectly loaded units during runs waste materials.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of cartoned units or shipping cases damaged during qualification runs
rows.imp_rat: Units/cases damaged during qualification runs is a validation-waste concern, marginal to the loading CFJ and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Qualification handling is generally controlled, keeping run damage relatively uncommon. Damage during qualification runs is not chiefly a time loss. Avoiding qualification-run damage relies on careful setup skill. Damaged qualification materials waste scarce representative samples at cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of qualification observations lacking links to the operator, equipment setting, packaging lot, condition, and source record
rows.imp_rat: Qualification observations lacking contextual links is a documentation-completeness error peripheral to physical loading; capped minimal by the batch ranking.
rows.sat_rat: Electronic records improve contextual linkage, but some observations still lack full traceability. Linking observations to operator, setting, and lot adds recording overhead. Maintaining full contextual traceability is a discipline task, not a skill scarcity. Unlinked observations force costly reconstruction.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of qualification delays caused by unavailable representative cartons, shipping cases, product units, or approved conditions
rows.imp_rat: Qualification delays from unavailable materials is a scheduling constraint, peripheral to physically loading cases and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Material staging planning helps but representative samples are still often unavailable on schedule. The loss is delay waiting for representative cartons, cases, product, or approved conditions. Little special skill is involved; it is a material-availability logistics issue. Idle qualification resources during waits carry cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production disruption caused by case-packing qualification studies
rows.imp_rat: Sterile production disruption from qualification studies is a cross-process delay, marginal to grouping/loading and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Scheduling around production helps, but qualification-driven disruption remains a real recurring hit. The core loss is production line downtime driven by qualification studies. Scheduling to minimize disruption needs planning skill but not scarce expertise. Fill-finish downtime is extremely expensive, making this loss primarily felt as lost capacity.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of challenged alarm, rejection, or recovery functions that fail to meet approved qualification criteria
rows.imp_rat: Alarm/rejection functions failing qualification criteria is a control-reliability concern, tangential to the loading CFJ and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Alarm/rejection challenge testing is a standard, well-developed practice, making this fairly well served. Challenging alarm and rejection functions is scheduled testing with limited time impact. Designing and executing challenge tests requires validation engineering skill. Failed reject functions escaping detection cause costly defects.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of product, packaging, equipment, software, operating, or facility changes implemented without validation impact assessment
rows.imp_rat: Changes implemented without validation impact assessment is a change-control defect, tangential to the physical load and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Change-control programs are mandatory and mature, so changes without impact assessment are relatively uncommon. Change impact assessment adds a control step but is procedurally bounded. Judging validation impact of changes requires experienced assessors. Unassessed changes cause costly compliance failures.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of case-packing drift before detection against validated acceptance criteria
rows.imp_rat: Duration of undetected packing drift is a monitoring-latency instability, marginal to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Monitoring against acceptance criteria exists but detection latency remains a real weakness. The core loss is the elapsed duration of drift before it is detected against criteria. Detecting drift early requires monitoring competence and attentiveness. Undetected drift produces accumulating defective output cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of ongoing-monitoring records containing missing, inconsistent, or untraceable case-packing evidence
rows.imp_rat: Monitoring records with untraceable evidence is a documentation defect, tangential to grouping/loading and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Ongoing-monitoring systems exist but missing or inconsistent records remain a recurring problem. Cleaning up monitoring records after the fact adds review time. Maintaining consistent traceable records needs discipline rather than scarce skill. Untraceable evidence forces costly reconstruction.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of post-maintenance or post-intervention setups that differ from the qualified case-packer configuration
rows.imp_rat: Post-maintenance setups differing from qualified config is a setup-consistency instability, peripheral to the loading CFJ and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Setup checklists exist but post-intervention configuration drift still occurs. Verifying post-maintenance setup against qualified config adds modest changeover time. Restoring qualified configuration after intervention depends on technician discipline and skill. Mis-setups cause scrap and re-runs at cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of deviations affecting the case-packer qualified state before approved resolution
rows.imp_rat: Deviation duration affecting qualified state is a resolution-timeliness delay, tangential to grouping/loading and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Deviation systems exist but resolution cycle times are chronically long. The primary loss is the duration deviations linger before approved resolution. Resolving deviations quickly draws on scarce quality and engineering attention. Prolonged deviations hold up production capacity at cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a case-packing qualification failure is attributed to the evaluator's recommendation
rows.imp_rat: A qualification failure attributed to the evaluator's recommendation is a reputational concern peripheral to the loading job; low consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with quality leadership when the evaluator's case-packing criteria are judged insufficiently rigorous
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when packing criteria are judged insufficiently rigorous is a reputational concern peripheral to physical loading; low consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the evaluator is bypassed in subsequent case-packing decisions after a disputed qualification recommendation
rows.imp_rat: Being bypassed after a disputed recommendation is an authority/reputation concern remote from the loading job; trivial stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that quality leadership views the evaluator's case-packer recommendation as purchase-driven rather than validation-driven
rows.imp_rat: Quality viewing a recommendation as purchase-driven is a reputational-perception concern peripheral to the loading job; low consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that auditors attribute an indefensible case-packer qualified state to the evaluator's validation-evidence criteria
rows.imp_rat: Auditors attributing an indefensible qualified state to the evaluator is a reputational concern peripheral to grouping cases; low consequence per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with regulatory reviewers when the evaluator's case-packing data-integrity judgment is challenged
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when a data-integrity judgment is challenged is a reputational concern peripheral to grouping cases; trivial stakes under the ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which auditors bypass the evaluator's judgment because case-packer evidence is not traceable to validated conditions
rows.imp_rat: Auditors bypassing the evaluator due to untraceable evidence is an authority/reputation concern remote from the loading job; trivial stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the evaluator is blamed for recommending case-packer qualification despite unresolved conflicting evidence
rows.imp_rat: Being blamed for recommending qualification despite conflicting evidence is a reputational concern peripheral to the loading job; low stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree to which acceptance criteria permit conflicting conclusions about each case-packer claim
rows.imp_rat: Criteria permitting conflicting conclusions is an acceptance-clarity ambiguity, tangential to the grouping/loading job and capped low.
rows.sat_rat: Poorly bounded criteria can allow inconsistent pass/fail conclusions, but mature protocol templates mitigate this to a degree. Ambiguous acceptance criteria cause re-argument that delays sign-off, but delay is secondary. Writing unambiguous, non-conflicting acceptance criteria is a specialized authoring skill and ambiguity arises directly from limited expertise in protocol design. Conflicting conclusions cause rework and re-review, a labor cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of case-packer claims lacking evidence traceable to test conditions and observed results
rows.imp_rat: Claims lacking traceable evidence is a documentation-attribution gap, tangential to the loading job and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Regulated validation frameworks (IQ/OQ/PQ, protocols) strongly enforce traceable evidence, so claims lacking traceability are relatively uncommon where GMP practice applies. Assembling traceable evidence adds documentation time to the validation effort. Producing evidence cleanly traceable from test condition to result requires disciplined documentation practice from skilled validation staff. Traceability documentation is labor cost with little material impact.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of validation delays caused by unavailable production windows, representative materials, interface specifications, or qualified personnel
rows.imp_rat: Validation delays from unavailable resources is a scheduling constraint, marginal to the grouping/loading CFJ and capped.
rows.sat_rat: Delays do not usually corrupt the outcome, so reliability is a lesser concern here. Unavailable production windows, materials, specs or personnel directly stall validation, and delay is precisely the loss this need addresses. Scheduling and coordinating scarce qualified personnel and resources is a planning burden dependent on experienced project management. Idle validation resources and extended timelines carry cost, though material loss is minimal.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of cartoned product and packaging material lost during validation runs
rows.imp_rat: Product/material lost during validation runs is a material-waste concern, peripheral to grouping/loading and held at the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Loss levels vary with how well runs are planned, but the outcome is consumed material rather than a data-integrity failure. Product and material loss during runs is a resource waste, not fundamentally a timing issue. Minimizing loss depends on careful run planning and skilled setup to avoid unnecessary scrap. Validation runs on sterile fill-finish products consume expensive product and packaging material, and this waste is the primary loss this need targets.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of doubt created by conflicting interpretations of upstream line-control and data-flow expectations
rows.imp_rat: Doubt from conflicting upstream interface interpretations is a coordination-ambiguity issue not central to loading cartons; low stakes per ordering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of anxiety while awaiting information needed to assess case-packer interface compatibility
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety awaiting interface-compatibility information is an emotional/coordination concern peripheral to loading cartons; minimal consequence.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of visible uncertainty when stakeholders request direction on an unresolved upstream interface question
rows.imp_rat: Visible uncertainty on an unresolved interface question is a coordination-timing issue peripheral to grouping cartons; trivial consequence per ranking.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling exposed when the case-packer recommendation relies on unsubstantiated line-control or data-flow assumptions
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed over unsubstantiated recommendation assumptions is an emotional/credibility error tangential to the CFJ; capped at 1.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of anxiety episodes while evidence of upstream line-control and data-flow compatibility remains incomplete
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety episodes while compatibility evidence is incomplete is an emotional/coordination worry peripheral to grouping cases; trivial consequence per ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the duration of doubt when qualification evidence conflicts with upstream control or data-flow signals
rows.imp_rat: Duration of doubt when qualification evidence conflicts with control signals is a review-timing issue not central to loading cartons; low consequence given the batch ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling exposed when qualification-readiness questions remain unresolved during recommendation review
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed over unresolved readiness questions is an emotional/credibility error, remote from physically packing cases; held at 1.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of doubt about which aggregation evidence supports the compliance claim when records conflict
rows.imp_rat: Doubt about which aggregation evidence supports a claim is a records-conflict ambiguity, tangential to physical loading and capped low.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of anxiety while awaiting clarification of unit-to-case aggregation exceptions
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety awaiting clarification of aggregation exceptions is an emotional/coordination worry remote from loading cartons; minimal stakes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of renewed alarm when previously resolved aggregation questions resurface before recommendation
rows.imp_rat: Renewed alarm when resolved aggregation questions resurface is a personal/coordination worry peripheral to loading cartons; minimal stakes.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a traceability inspection finding tied to the recommended case-packing approach is publicly attributed to the evaluation owner
rows.imp_rat: An inspection finding publicly attributed to the owner is a reputational concern peripheral to physical loading; held minimal by the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of credibility lost with Quality Assurance and Regulatory Affairs when the traceability rationale fails formal challenge
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when traceability rationale fails challenge is a reputational concern peripheral to grouping cases; held minimal by the ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which packaging engineering or procurement excludes the evaluation owner from revisions affecting case-level traceability
rows.imp_rat: Excluding the evaluation owner from traceability revisions is a governance/coordination error with indirect job impact; both proxies capped at 1.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which Quality Assurance or Regulatory Affairs second-guesses the evaluation owner's case-level traceability judgments
rows.imp_rat: QA second-guessing traceability judgments is an evaluation-reputation concern peripheral to grouping cartons; held minimal by the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that engineering or line-control leadership publicly attributes a case-packer handoff failure to the recommendation owner
rows.imp_rat: Public attribution of a handoff failure to the owner is a reputational concern peripheral to physical loading; held minimal by the ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of credibility lost with engineering and line-control leadership when case-packer interface assumptions prove incomplete
rows.imp_rat: Credibility lost when interface assumptions prove incomplete is a reputational concern peripheral to physical loading; held minimal by the ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of case-packer integration recommendations publicly overruled by engineering or line-control leadership
rows.imp_rat: Integration recommendations publicly overruled is an authority/reputation concern peripheral to physical loading; held minimal by the ordering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which engineering or line-control leadership excludes the recommendation owner from subsequent case-packer integration decisions
rows.imp_rat: Being excluded from subsequent integration decisions is an authority/reputation concern peripheral to physical loading; held minimal by ceiling.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree to which projected labor, throughput, and loss-reduction benefits exceed evidence-supported benefits
rows.imp_rat: Benefits exceeding evidence is a business-case ambiguity remote from packing execution; ceiling holds both at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Benefit projections are commonly optimistic and weakly evidenced, a persistent unreliability. Overstated benefits are exposed only after deployment, not a timing issue. Grounding benefit claims in evidence requires analytical discipline often lacking. Overstated labor and loss-reduction benefits inflate ROI and misdirect spend.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of acquisition, integration, qualification, operating, maintenance, and end-of-life costs omitted from the financial model
rows.imp_rat: Lifecycle costs omitted from the financial model is a finance-completeness error upstream of packing; minimal consequence under the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Financial models are commonly incomplete because no standard practice captures all lifecycle categories reliably. Rebuilding models when costs are missed consumes review cycles. Full total-cost-of-ownership modeling demands specialist financial and technical skill often lacking at the buyer level. Omitting operating, maintenance and end-of-life costs from the model understates true spend, a persistent gap.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the comparison baseline misstates current case-packing costs, labor use, throughput, downtime, or losses
rows.imp_rat: Misstated comparison baseline is a business-case defect remote from physically packing cases; held at 1 on both.
rows.sat_rat: Current case-packing baselines are frequently unmeasured or estimated, making the comparison unreliable. A wrong baseline is discovered slowly during review, secondarily. Establishing accurate current-state costs and losses needs data and analytical skill often lacking. A misstated baseline distorts the entire investment justification and cost comparison.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved differences in demand, throughput, labor, downtime, and loss assumptions among finance, production, quality, and procurement
rows.imp_rat: Unresolved assumption differences among functions is a planning-ambiguity error remote from packing; ceiling forces 1 on both.
rows.sat_rat: Assumption differences among functions commonly persist unreconciled into the financial case. Reconciling divergent assumptions late slows review. Harmonizing demand and throughput assumptions across functions needs analytical coordination. Divergent assumptions lead to mis-sized, mis-costed acquisitions.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of error in cash-flow timing assumptions caused by uncertain qualification and production-release dates
rows.imp_rat: Cash-flow timing error from uncertain release dates is a financial-modeling error remote from packing; ceiling holds both at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Qualification and release dates are inherently uncertain, so cash-flow timing assumptions are frequently wrong. Uncertain qualification dates are the source, but the tracked error is cash-flow timing accuracy. Estimating qualification and release dates needs project and regulatory judgement. Wrong cash-flow timing misstates financing cost and return.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of exposure from deposits, payment milestones, escalation, currency, and taxes omitted from the financial case
rows.imp_rat: Exposure from deposits/taxes omitted from the financial case is a finance-completeness concern upstream of packing; minimal consequence per ordering.
rows.sat_rat: Financial cases inconsistently capture escalation and currency terms, so surprises recur. Discovering these exposures late delays re-approval but the harm is financial. Modeling deposits, escalation, currency and tax needs finance specialist input. These commercial exposures are frequently omitted, leaving real unbudgeted cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that acquisition, qualification, and ramp-up spending exceeds the financial-case estimate because downside scenarios are underrepresented
rows.imp_rat: Spending exceeding estimate from underrepresented downside is a financial-planning error distant from the job; capped at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Financial cases routinely omit downside scenarios, so overruns beyond estimate are common and poorly guarded. Underestimated ramp-up surfaces late, but the harm is estimate accuracy. Building downside scenarios requires risk-modeling skill rarely applied rigorously. Underrepresented downside means actual spend exceeds the case, a recurring overrun.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of funding-review delays caused by missing evidence or untraceable assumptions
rows.imp_rat: Funding-review delays from missing evidence is an authorization-timing error peripheral to the CFJ; both proxies at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Missing or untraceable assumptions routinely stall reviews, and evidence discipline is inconsistent. Delays caused by missing evidence directly extend funding review, the core harm. Preparing traceable assumptions needs analytical documentation discipline. Rework to supply missing evidence consumes effort and delays spend.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of spending for required qualification and documentation work excluded from supplier scope
rows.imp_rat: Excluding required documentation work from supplier scope is a procurement defect tangential to execution; both proxies capped at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Scope boundaries for qualification are commonly unclear, so exclusions recur. Excluded qualification work is discovered late, delaying release secondarily. Scoping qualification and documentation into supplier terms needs quality expertise. Qualification and documentation work excluded from supplier scope becomes unbudgeted internal cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount by which site and production-line integration spending exceeds its budget allowance
rows.imp_rat: Integration spending exceeding its budget is a finance-overrun concern upstream of packing; minimal stakes under the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Integration cost estimates are notoriously unreliable and commonly blown, with weak corrective practice. Integration overruns extend projects but the loss is budgetary. Estimating line-integration cost requires engineering expertise the buyer depends on others for. Site and line integration routinely exceeds allowance, a frequent and sizable overspend.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of inconsistency in included scope and commercial terms across supplier offers
rows.imp_rat: Inconsistent scope/terms across offers is a procurement-ambiguity error remote from execution; capped at 1 on both.
rows.sat_rat: Supplier offers routinely differ in included scope and terms, making reliable comparison hard without normalization templates that are inconsistently applied. Inconsistent offers slow comparison, secondarily. Normalizing scope across offers needs analytical procurement skill. Inconsistent scope leads to apples-to-oranges pricing and hidden costs.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of added acquisition spending caused by case-packing requirement changes after contract award
rows.imp_rat: Added acquisition spend from post-award requirement changes is a procurement rework cost distant from packing execution; ceiling forces 1 on both.
rows.sat_rat: Change-control clauses exist but requirement instability makes added cost a recurring outcome. Change-driven rework can delay delivery but the tracked harm is added spend. Managing change orders and pricing them fairly requires contracting skill. Post-award requirement changes reliably trigger costly variations, a frequent overrun source.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage by which supplier pricing increases between capital approval and contract commitment

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of unbudgeted expenditure for site modifications and sterile fill-finish line integration
rows.imp_rat: Unbudgeted site-integration spend is a project-cost waste peripheral to the packing job; both proxies at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Integration scope for sterile fill-finish is often underspecified, so unbudgeted costs recur. Scoping site-mod and integration cost early adds engineering time. Estimating sterile line integration cost requires specialized facilities and validation expertise. Unbudgeted site modification and integration is a frequent source of overrun.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of case-packer acquisition expenditure above the authorized allowance before acceptance
rows.imp_rat: Expenditure above the authorized allowance before acceptance is a finance-overrun concern distant from grouping cartons; minimal consequence per ordering.
rows.sat_rat: Purchase controls and acceptance milestones reliably catch most overruns, though scope creep still leaks through. Change-order and acceptance controls add administrative steps but are routine. Managing spend against authorized allowance uses standard procurement discipline. Overspend above the authorized allowance is the direct waste this need targets, partly contained by PO controls.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of case-packer qualification and compliance expenditure above the approved provision
rows.imp_rat: Qualification/compliance overspend above provision is a budget-control error, not central to loading cases; both proxies held at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Compliance-cost provisions are frequently underestimated in fill-finish contexts, so overruns recur. Estimating qualification and compliance effort early takes time the review often lacks. Scoping sterile qualification cost demands validation-cost expertise that is scarce. Qualification/compliance overrun above provision is direct unplanned expenditure.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of recurring labor, utility, and packaging-consumable expenditure above financial-case assumptions
rows.imp_rat: Recurring operating spend above assumptions is a budget-control waste remote from grouping/loading cases; held at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Operating-cost assumptions are established practice but frequently drift from actuals. Estimating recurring operating cost is a standard but time-consuming input. Building accurate labor, utility and consumable assumptions needs operational costing skill. Recurring spend above assumptions directly erodes the realized financial case.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of maintenance, spare-part, and service expenditure above the case-packer lifecycle forecast
rows.imp_rat: Maintenance/spare-part overspend above forecast is a budget-control waste peripheral to loading cases; ceiling holds both at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Lifecycle service forecasts are commonly optimistic, so overruns recur across ownership. Forecasting maintenance and spares over life takes upfront effort. Estimating service and spare-part cost needs reliability-engineering input often not sought. Maintenance overrun above forecast is direct recurring unplanned expenditure.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish line disruption attributable to case-packer installation, qualification, and startup
rows.imp_rat: Line disruption from installation/startup affects uptime but ranking caps it; relevance to the job and severity both held at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Disruption from install/qual is hard to predict and poorly bounded in typical plans. Line disruption during install and qualification is a significant, often underestimated downtime. Minimizing sterile-line disruption needs specialized commissioning and validation expertise. Production disruption on a sterile line carries high lost-output cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of unplanned case-packing stoppages during the ownership period
rows.imp_rat: Unplanned stoppages during ownership directly interrupt the job, but the batch ceiling forces relevance and severity to 1.
rows.sat_rat: Preventive maintenance practices exist but unplanned stoppages remain a recurring operational reality. Unplanned stoppages during ownership cause lost run time, addressed reactively. Reducing stoppages needs reliability engineering and maintenance capability that varies by site. Each stoppage carries lost-output and response cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of unamortized investment remaining when the case packer no longer meets site requirements
rows.imp_rat: Unamortized investment on obsolescence is a financial-write-off waste distant from packing execution; held at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Obsolescence timing is poorly forecast, so residual write-off risk is only loosely controlled. Estimating useful life against requirements takes forward-looking analysis time. Judging when the asset stops meeting requirements needs lifecycle and strategy foresight. Unamortized value at obsolescence is a direct write-off loss.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of financial assumptions in the case-packer rationale lacking traceable evidence
rows.imp_rat: Assumptions lacking traceable evidence is a business-case ambiguity remote from execution; ceiling holds both at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Evidence traceability for assumptions is weak, so many assumptions lack documented backing. Tracing evidence for each assumption adds documentation time. Establishing traceable evidence requires analytical discipline often not applied. Untraceable assumptions risk decisions on unfounded numbers.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of misalignment between projected case-packing capacity and the site capacity strategy
rows.imp_rat: Misalignment of projected capacity with site strategy is a planning-analysis concern upstream of the loading job; low stakes under the ceiling.
rows.sat_rat: Capacity planning inputs exist but reconciliation to strategy is inconsistent, leaving recurring misalignment risk. Aligning projected capacity to the capacity strategy involves back-and-forth with planning that extends the review window. Translating a site capacity strategy into required case-packing throughput needs cross-functional planning judgement in short supply. Misalignment risks funding the wrong capacity, an expensive miss, though the check itself is low-cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of investment value left unquantified across capacity, labor, and production-continuity benefits
rows.imp_rat: Unquantified investment value is a business-case ambiguity distant from the job; relevance and severity held at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Benefit quantification is inconsistently complete, so value is frequently left unstated. Capturing all benefit streams lengthens the business-case build. Valuing capacity, labor and continuity benefits requires benefits-quantification skill unevenly present. Unquantified value understates the true return and risks under-justifying worthwhile spend.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of material product-quality, traceability, and validation risks absent from the investment rationale
rows.imp_rat: Missing product-quality/traceability risks in rationale is a business-case gap; ranking forces relevance and severity to 1.
rows.sat_rat: Quality-risk capture in investment rationale is inconsistent, leaving material risks unstated. Surfacing quality and traceability risks adds review effort. Identifying product-quality and validation risks requires quality-assurance expertise not resident in procurement. Absent quality risks can lead to expensive post-hoc fixes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of decision-relevant investment alternatives evaluated on inconsistent financial bases
rows.imp_rat: Alternatives evaluated on inconsistent bases is an analysis-ambiguity error distant from packing; capped at 1 on both.
rows.sat_rat: Consistent evaluation frameworks are inconsistently enforced across alternatives. Standardizing evaluation bases adds harmonization time. Ensuring consistent financial bases across alternatives requires financial-analysis rigor. Inconsistent bases can misdirect capital to a weaker option.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicting financial and capacity figures presented to executive stakeholders
rows.imp_rat: Conflicting figures to executives is a reporting-ambiguity error distant from the packing job; both proxies at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Version control on figures is inconsistent, so conflicting numbers reach executives fairly often. Reconciling conflicting figures for executives adds review rework time. Producing a single reconciled view requires financial-consolidation discipline. Conflicting figures risk a decision on wrong numbers rather than direct cost.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of variation in projected returns across credible demand, throughput, and operating-cost conditions
rows.imp_rat: Variation in projected returns across conditions is an investment-analysis concern distant from loading cartons; low stakes per the ordering.
rows.sat_rat: Scenario analysis is inconsistently applied, so projected returns are often presented as point estimates with hidden variability. Running multi-scenario return analyses adds analytical cycles to the review. Sensitivity and scenario modeling across demand and cost conditions demands financial-modeling expertise seldom held by the executor. Unexamined return variation risks funding a project that underperforms, exposing invested capital.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration of final approval delay caused by unresolved executive objections
rows.imp_rat: Approval delay from executive objections is an authorization-timing error tangential to the job; both proxies at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Pre-alignment practices help but late executive objections still unpredictably stall sign-off. Unresolved executive objections directly stall final approval, a recurring delay. Preempting objections requires stakeholder-management and persuasion skill. Approval delay defers spend authority but has limited direct cost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of financial and capacity impact from deferring the case-packer investment left unquantified
rows.imp_rat: Unquantified deferral impact is a planning-ambiguity error remote from the packing job; ceiling caps relevance and severity at 1.
rows.sat_rat: Deferral-cost analysis is largely absent from standard practice, leaving this need chronically unserved. Quantifying deferral impact takes analysis rarely prioritized under time pressure. Modeling the cost of delay requires economic-analysis skill seldom applied to deferral scenarios. Unquantified deferral impact hides real financial and capacity costs of waiting.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of return forgone by funding case packing instead of the best competing capital option
rows.imp_rat: Forgone return versus competing capital is a portfolio-allocation waste far from execution; capped at 1 on both.
rows.sat_rat: Opportunity-cost comparison is inconsistently performed, leaving this only partly managed. Comparing against the best alternative adds analytical time. Assessing opportunity cost requires portfolio-economics judgement rarely applied thoroughly. Forgone return on a better option is real economic loss even if invisible on the ledger.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of funded case-packing capacity left unused over the investment horizon
rows.imp_rat: Unused funded capacity is a utilization waste peripheral to the packing job; capped at 1 on both.
rows.sat_rat: Utilization forecasting is inconsistent, so stranded-capacity risk is only loosely managed. Projecting utilization over the horizon adds analytical time. Forecasting utilization needs demand and capacity modeling skill. Unused funded capacity is idle capital — a direct waste.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that another line constraint prevents added case-packing capacity from increasing saleable site output
rows.imp_rat: Another line constraint preventing added capacity from increasing output is a throughput-planning concern distant from grouping cases; minimal stakes.
rows.sat_rat: Whole-line constraint analysis is inconsistently done, so this failure mode recurs unnoticed until after install. Line-balancing studies to expose other constraints take time that reviews rarely allow. Identifying whether an upstream/downstream bottleneck negates added capacity needs line-engineering expertise. Funding case-packing capacity that cannot lift saleable output wastes the entire investment's intended return.

rows.stmt: Reduce the duration between capital deployment and realization of expected case-packing benefits
